terresAgricoles.asbestosHint: Fibre-cement roofing, old insulation — mandatory survey
terresAgricoles.sourceDisclaimer: Source: estimates based on observed transactions. Indicative data — the Luxembourg agricultural land market has low transparency.

seo.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions about agricultural land
seo.evaluationContent: Agricultural land valuation in Luxembourg follows specific rules. Average prices range from EUR 30,000 to 80,000/ha in 2025, with peaks above EUR 100,000/ha near developable zones.
seo.prixZonesTitle: Prices by geographical zone and soil quality
seo.prixZonesContent: Prices vary by zone: Centre-South EUR 50,000-80,000/ha, Centre EUR 40,000-60,000/ha, North EUR 30,000-50,000/ha, East (Moselle) EUR 45,000-70,000/ha.
seo.facteursContent: Key factors: reclassification potential, existing rural lease (15-30% discount), existing buildings, accessibility and parcel shape.
seo.bailRuralContent: The rural lease is governed by the law of 2 July 1969, with strong tenant protections including minimum 6-year duration and pre-emption rights.
seo.faq1q: What is the average price per hectare in Luxembourg?
seo.faq1a: Between EUR 30,000 and 80,000/ha depending on zone and quality, with peaks above EUR 100,000/ha for reclassification potential.
seo.faq2q: How is soil quality assessed?
seo.faq2a: According to the Luxembourg pedological classification (classes I to V).
seo.faq3q: Does an active rural lease affect the price?
seo.faq3a: Yes, a 15-30% discount is typical due to legal tenant protections.
seo.faq4q: Can agricultural land be reclassified as buildable?
seo.faq4a: Yes, during PAG revision. Reclassification multiplies value by 10-50x but involves lengthy procedures.

terresAgricoles.vitiIntro: Specific reference for LU Moselle vineyards (Schengen → Stadtbredimus). Value depends heavily on varietal, exposure, and planting rights.
terresAgricoles.vitiSources: Sources: Institut Viti-Vinicole (IVV) Remich, Wine & Crémant Promotion Commission, private transactions 2023-2025. Potential IVV subsidies: planting aid, restructuring, organic support. AOP planting rights are strictly capped.
terresAgricoles.mapSubtitle: €/hectare prices by region and crop type — estimates based on 2023-2025 transactions (Rural Economy Service, ACT, IVV).
terresAgricoles.mapDisclaimer: Indicative estimates. Actual prices vary ±20% depending on plot, soil quality, access, planting rights (vineyard), adjoining buildings. Consult the Rural Economy Service for expertise.

vefa.title: VEFA Simulator
vefa.subtitle: Off-Plan Purchase — payment schedule, VAT, registration duties, completion guarantee
vefa.prixTotalHint: All-inclusive contractual price excluding acquisition costs
vefa.residencePrincipaleHint: Grants reduced 3% VAT and Bëllegen Akt credit
vefa.tauxHypothequeHint: For calculating intercalary interest during construction
vefa.echeancierHint: Adjust percentages to match your VEFA contract. Total must equal 100%.
vefa.dureeEstimee: Estimated duration: {mois} months from contract to delivery. Dates are indicative and depend on actual construction progress.
vefa.echeancierNote: Indicative schedule based on construction progress. Payment calls are issued by the developer upon certification of each milestone by an independent architect.
vefa.interetsIntercalairesDesc: During construction, the bank releases the loan progressively. The buyer pays interest on the capital already disbursed, without repaying principal. This is the #1 hidden cost of an off-plan purchase.
vefa.coutIntercalaireResume: Estimated intercalary cost: {montant} over the entire construction period ({taux}% annual on a loan of {pret}). This amount is added to the total acquisition cost and represents {pctPrix} of the property price.
vefa.garantieObligationText: The developer must provide a completion guarantee (also called extrinsic guarantee) issued by an approved financial institution in Luxembourg. This guarantee ensures the property will be completed even if the developer defaults.
vefa.garantieProtectionText: In a VEFA purchase, funds paid by the buyer are protected. The notarial deed must mention the guarantee and its conditions. Payment calls cannot exceed the percentages set by law, linked to actual construction progress.
vefa.garantieReceptionText: At delivery, the buyer may note reservations about defects found. The developer has a period to remedy them. The ten-year structural warranty covers structural defects for 10 years.
vefa.bonTvaText: The tax benefit is capped at {plafond}. Beyond that, the remaining VAT is charged at 17%. The benefit is granted only once in the buyer's lifetime and must be applied for at the Registration, Domains and VAT Administration (AED).
vefa.bonTerrainText: In a VEFA purchase, the land portion is subject to registration duties (7%), while the construction portion is subject to VAT. The split is set in the notarial deed.
vefa.bonBellegenText: Tax credit of EUR 40,000 per buyer (EUR 80,000 for a couple) on registration duties. Only applicable for the primary residence and on first use.
vefa.bonInteretsText: During the construction phase (24 to 30 months), the bank releases the loan as payment calls are made. The buyer pays monthly interest on the capital already disbursed, without amortising the principal. This often-forgotten cost typically amounts to EUR 15,000 to 25,000 for a standard loan.
vefa.bonDureeText: Expect an average of 24 to 30 months between signing and delivery. The timeline depends on project size, weather and contractor availability.

seo.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions about VEFA in Luxembourg
seo.guideTitle: Complete guide to off-plan purchases (VEFA) in Luxembourg
seo.guideContent:
VEFA (Vente en l'Etat Futur d'Achevement) is the preferred acquisition method for new housing in Luxembourg. Governed by the law of 18 April 2018, it provides a protective legal framework for buyers:

  • Reservation contract: preliminary document with maximum 5% deposit
  • Notarised sale deed: progressive ownership transfer with regulated payment calls
  • Completion guarantee: mandatory bank or insurance guarantee
  • Decennial guarantee: 10-year builder liability for hidden defects
seo.echeancierSeoContent:
Article 1601-11 sets a maximum payment schedule:

  • Sale deed: max 33%
  • Watertight stage: cumulative max 60%
  • Completion: cumulative max 95%
  • Delivery: 5% balance (retained if defects noted)
seo.tvaSeoTitle: VAT and taxation of VEFA in Luxembourg
seo.tvaSeoContent:
The VEFA tax regime offers significant advantages:

  • Land portion: 7% registration duties with Bellegen Akt for primary residence
  • Construction portion: 3% VAT (super-reduced) for primary residence, capped at EUR 50,000 in savings
  • Beyond cap or for investment: 17% VAT
seo.intercalairesTitle: Interim interest: an often underestimated cost
seo.intercalairesContent: Interim interest is paid on funds already released during construction, before capital repayment begins. For a EUR 600,000 VEFA with EUR 500,000 loan at 3.8% over 18 months of construction, interim interest is approximately EUR 12,000-15,000.
seo.faq1q: What is VEFA in Luxembourg?
seo.faq1a: VEFA is a contract where the developer sells a property not yet built or under construction. The buyer progressively becomes owner with regulated staged payments under the law of 18 April 2018.
seo.faq2q: What are the risks of VEFA?
seo.faq2a: Main risks are delivery delays (contractual penalties), specification changes (developer cannot change essential elements without agreement), and developer insolvency (covered by mandatory completion guarantee).
seo.faq3q: How do I benefit from the 3% reduced VAT rate?
seo.faq3a: The 3% rate applies to the construction portion of property intended as primary residence. The application is made to the AED, typically through the notary.
seo.faq4q: What if there are defects at delivery?
seo.faq4a: At reception, prepare a detailed report listing all defects. The 5% balance is retained until defects are fixed. The developer must correct within a reasonable timeframe.
seo.faq5a: Yes, interim interest is deductible from taxable income like mortgage interest, within the caps for primary residence (EUR 1,500-3,000/year).

vefa.retardChantierHint: If delivery is delayed, the bank has already released the full loan: intercalary interest keeps accruing on the full capital until the completion guarantee is released and amortization begins.
vefa.gfaSubtitle: Mandatory for VEFA developments. Protects buyers against developer default.
vefa.gfaPrime: GFA premium rate (% of total price)
vefa.gfaExtrinsequeNote: Premium paid to developer by buyer via sale price. Bank guarantee or insurance covering completion cost. LU standard.
vefa.gfaIntrinsequeNote: No external premium — developer proves strength (own funds ≥ 75 % of construction cost, no bank loan). Rarer, used for small projects.

faq.title: Questions common to all 6 profiles

items.q: How much does the tevaxia subscription cost?
items.a: No per-module or per-user billing within each profile. A single tevaxia subscription gives access to all tools for your role, without quotas or volume limits. Full pricing on the /pricing page.

items.q: Can I request custom development?
items.a: Yes. A specific format (convocation, report, accounting export), an atypical calculation (custom weighting, proprietary business rule), a dedicated integration: development in 1 to 3 weeks depending on complexity. Free quote.

items.q: What's the support response time?
items.a: Average response under 6 hours on business days, maximum 24 hours. Email contact@tevaxia.lu. No chatbot, no numbered tickets: you speak with a human who knows the product.

items.q: Is data hosted in the EU?
items.a: Yes. Supabase (AWS eu-central-1, Frankfurt) with user-level Row Level Security, HTTPS in transit, GDPR compliant. Full data export to ZIP at any time (GDPR article 20).

items.q: How do I migrate from another tool?
items.a: CSV or Excel import depending on data type. Automatic column mapping with preview. Free migration assistance: we help you import the first dataset.

items.q: What's the release cadence?
items.a: Releases every 2 weeks on average. Public backlog on github.com/Tevaxia, your requests are visible and can be prioritised. Auto-updated: no version to install, everything runs SaaS.

items.q: Is tevaxia multilingual?
items.a: Yes, 5 languages: French, English, German, Portuguese and Luxembourgish. Every page, every PDF, every notification is available in all 5 languages. Persona landings are currently complete in FR and EN, with DE/LB/PT being translated.

items.q: Can tevaxia be used outside Luxembourg?
items.a: The platform is primarily calibrated for the Luxembourg legal framework (3% VAT, Bëllegen Akt, 5% rule, TEGOVA). Some modules also work for France and Belgium (Factur-X, DCF, buy/rent comparator). For cross-border usage, contact us to confirm coverage.

items.q: Does the SHA-256 signature have legal weight?
items.a: The SHA-256 signature guarantees document integrity. It does not replace a legally qualified eIDAS signature, but provides cryptographic proof that the report was not altered after issuance.

items.a: Dedicated section: you list binding assumptions, the tool displays the mandatory EVS 5 warning and adjusts value based on inputs.

problem.title: Computing LU yield means juggling 8 different simulators

items.desc: 7% duties, transcription, Bëllegen Akt (up to 40,000 € deduction), notary, 3% VAT new-build: many investors compute wrong and block their financing plan.

items.title: Gross vs net yield confusion
items.desc: Between gross, net, and net-net (after tax), numbers don't mean the same thing. A 5% gross yield can end at 2% after taxes.

items.desc: Online Excel simulators are calibrated for FR or BE. No 5% rule, no LU commercial lease, no LU 10% capital gains.

items.q: Is Bëllegen Akt deduction properly handled?
items.a: Yes, the tool checks eligibility conditions and applies up to 40,000 € per person deduction on registration duties.

items.q: Is 3% new-build VAT properly included?
items.a: Yes, the VEFA simulator automatically applies the 3% rate subject to primary residence occupied 2 years minimum.

items.q: Does the multi-tenant DCF handle French and LU breaks?
items.a: Yes. Each lease can have its duration, indexation (IRL, ILAT, ILC, IPC), scheduled step rents, break options, planned CAPEX.

items.q: Are LU capital gains properly calculated?
items.a: Yes. Primary residence exemption, secondary residence half-global rate, investment global/half-global rate depending on holding period.

items.q: Do banking tools (LTV, DSCR, CRR) follow CSSF rules?
items.a: Yes. LTV max by borrower profile, DSCR target 1.25, CRR mortgage lending value with energy haircut. +2% stress test.

items.q: Can I share my simulations with my banker?
items.a: Yes. Dedicated Financing file PDF. View-only sharing option with limited duration link (7 to 90 days).

seo.faqTitle: FAQ — Co-ownership Management in Luxembourg
seo.gestionTitle: Co-ownership management in Luxembourg: the role of the syndic
seo.gestionContent:
In Luxembourg, the management of a co-owned building is governed by the law of 16 May 1975 on co-ownership of buildings. Professional syndics (such as BRUNATA, Leximmo, ImmoConcept) handle the administrative, technical and financial management on behalf of the co-owners' assembly.

Their responsibilities include: preparing the budget forecast, distributing charges according to shares, maintaining common areas, arranging insurance, and keeping accounts. The syndic convenes and chairs general assemblies and implements voted decisions.

Our digital dashboard enables syndics to efficiently simulate and present energy renovation scenarios, a central challenge given EPBD obligations.
seo.chargesTitle: Co-ownership charges distribution: rules and best practices
seo.chargesContent:
Common charges distribution in Luxembourg co-ownership follows precise rules:

  • General charges (art. 6, law 1975): distributed according to co-ownership shares proportional to the relative value of each private lot.
  • Special charges: certain charges may be distributed by utility (elevator by floor, heating by area).
  • Reserve fund: building a reserve fund for major works is recommended (minimum 5% of annual budget).
  • Transparency: the syndic must present detailed annual accounts and the forecast budget at the GA.
seo.renovationTitle: Energy renovation in co-ownership: obligations and subsidies
seo.renovationContent:
Energy renovation of co-owned buildings has become a major issue in Luxembourg:

  • EPBD Directive 2024/1275: the worst-performing residential buildings must be renovated on a progressive timeline (class E by 2030, class D by 2033).
  • Klimabonus / PRIMe House: Luxembourg's subsidy programme covers 30-50% of renovation costs for insulation, heating, windows and renewables.
  • GA vote: energy renovation works require a qualified majority of 2/3 of shares (improvement works) or simple majority (necessary maintenance).
  • Energy advice: myenergy offers free support for co-ownerships wishing to establish a renovation plan.
seo.reglementationContent:
The legal framework applicable to co-ownerships in Luxembourg includes:

  • Law of 16 May 1975: co-ownership status for buildings — defines rules, rights and obligations of co-owners, the role of the syndic.
  • Grand-ducal regulation of 13 June 1975: implementing measures of the 1975 law.
  • Law of 21 September 2006: residential lease — regulates rents and recoverable charges.
  • Grand-ducal regulation of 5 May 2023: PRIMe House financial aids for energy renovation.
  • EPBD Directive 2024/1275: energy performance of buildings — renovation deadlines.
seo.faq1A: Shares are calculated proportionally to the relative value of each private lot compared to the total building value. This value considers the area, location (floor, exposure), and consistency of the lot. They are set in the co-ownership regulations and can only be modified by unanimous vote.
seo.faq2Q: What majorities are needed to vote on energy renovation works?
seo.faq2A: Routine maintenance works can be voted by simple majority (50% + 1 of shares). Improvement works (including deep energy renovation) require a qualified majority of 2/3 of represented shares. Works modifying the building's purpose require unanimity.
seo.faq3Q: How to finance the energy renovation of a co-ownership in Luxembourg?
seo.faq3A: Financing combines: the reserve fund, extraordinary contributions, collective loans taken by the syndicate, and Klimabonus/PRIMe House subsidies. Luxembourg banks offer dedicated co-ownership loans. myenergy provides free support for co-ownerships in their approach.
seo.faq4Q: What are the syndic's obligations regarding energy?
seo.faq4A: The syndic must maintain the energy performance certificate (EPC), inform co-owners of EPBD obligations, propose necessary works at the GA to meet regulatory targets, and support Klimabonus subsidy applications. They are responsible for monitoring energy consumption of common areas.

seo.contextTitle: Hotel pre-acquisition: which analyses before signing?
seo.contextContent: Acquiring a hotel or motel differs fundamentally from residential real estate: value depends on the operation (RevPAR, GOP, EBITDA), not only on surface area. An informed decision requires six analytical angles: income-based valuation, debt coverage, operating forecast, energy compliance, competitive positioning, and — for international buyers — E-2 visa eligibility.
seo.methodTitle: Method: combining income and comparables approaches
seo.methodContent: Professional hotel valuation blends two methods. The income approach discounts stabilised EBITDA at a capitalisation rate reflecting operational risk (5-9% depending on category). The comparables approach applies a price per key observed on recent transactions (€40k-€350k by segment). The retained value is typically a weighted average.
seo.usaliTitle: USALI: the common language of hotel P&L
seo.usaliContent: The Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry (11th ed., 2014) standardises hotel P&L presentation worldwide: Rooms / Food & Beverage / Other Operating Departments, then fixed charges, FF&E reserve (4% standard) down to EBITDA. A buyer reasoning in USALI communicates in a standardised way with banks, appraisers and operators.
seo.faq1q: What does the Hospitality Solutions hub cover?
seo.faq1a: Two complementary sides. (1) Day-to-day operations (PMS): bookings, rooms, rate plans, calendar, check-in/out, 3% VAT invoicing, guests (GDPR CRM), ADR/RevPAR reports, iCal export. (2) Financial analysis and decision support: RevPAR/EBITDA valuation combining DCF + comparables, DSCR & financing, USALI P&L, RevPAR vs market (MPI/ARI/RGI), group benchmark, E-2 visa score, 50-point due diligence, energy retrofit with Klimabonus for the tertiary sector.
seo.faq2q: Is the PMS compliant with Luxembourg tax rules?
seo.faq2a: Yes. Lodging VAT 3% (art. 40 LU VAT law 12.02.1979 + annex B), F&B VAT 17% (or 14% / 8% depending on products), commune-level tourist tax configurable per property (€3/night/adult in Luxembourg-City in 2026), invoice immutable once issued (art. 61-63 VAT law + grand-ducal regulation 24.03.2023 on e-invoicing). Guest data follows GDPR with scheduled deletion 3 years after the last stay, except tax obligations (10 years — art. 16 AO).
seo.faq3q: Can I sync with Booking, Airbnb, Expedia?
seo.faq3a: Yes via a read-only iCal feed (RFC 5545) per property or per room. You paste it into your Booking / Airbnb / Expedia extranet so they block occupied dates. We don't push inventory nor run a bidirectional channel manager — this is intentional: it avoids overbookings caused by desync and stays within the limits of free self-serve APIs.
seo.faq4q: What is the difference between GOP, EBITDA and net income?
seo.faq4a: GOP (Gross Operating Profit) = total revenues − operating charges; the operator's performance indicator. EBITDA = GOP − FF&E reserve (4% USALI 11th ed.); the debt-service capacity indicator used by banks. Net income = EBITDA − interest − depreciation − taxes; the accounting result.
seo.faq5q: Do the tools work for a hotel in France, Belgium or Germany?
seo.faq5a: The financial analysis, yes: RevPAR, EBITDA, DSCR, USALI and E-2 visa are international standards. The PMS, however, defaults to LU tax rules (3% VAT + commune tourist tax) — usable cross-border by adjusting the VAT rates per property, but not yet auto-configured for other countries. Klimabonus and renovation aids are strictly Luxembourgish.
seo.faq6q: Do I need a TEGOVA expert for a bank?
seo.faq6a: For a secured hotel loan (>€1M), most LU/BE banks require a TEGOVA EVS 2025 report signed by a qualified appraiser. Our tools allow pre-qualification of a file before ordering that expert work. The /valorisation EVS module generates a SHA-256 signable report with a public verification block on /verify.

seo.usageTitle: When a co-branded estimation report changes the game
seo.usageContent: A prospecting meeting is often won in the first 15 minutes. Arriving with an estimation file in your agency's brand — including acquisition fees and applicable aids for the prospect's profile — transforms the conversation: you talk precise figures, Luxembourg legal framework, realistic financial path. Winning the mandate becomes natural.
seo.brandingContent: The PDF carries your logo, your agent's contact details and your customisable legal mention. Calculations remain powered by our up-to-date engines: Observatoire de l'Habitat prices, Bëllegen Akt rates, Klimabonus caps, 5% rule. You build on our technical expertise while keeping the client relationship.
seo.conformiteContent: Our tax and regulatory base is updated as soon as official publications are released (Mémorial A, CSSF communications, ACD circulars). Your reports automatically reflect the latest evolutions — the 3.5% temporary rate revision, annual Klimabonus scales, income thresholds for state aids.
seo.faq1q: How many agent accounts per agency?
seo.faq1a: Up to 30 users per agency account, sharing a single estimation quota, a single brand chart and an admin console to track activity per user.
seo.faq2q: Can I customise the legal mention on each report?
seo.faq2a: Yes. Each agency defines its unique legal mention (RCS, accreditation, professional card…) that appears at the bottom of every PDF generated by its agents.
seo.faq3q: Is the report available in 5 languages?
seo.faq3a: Yes. FR / EN / DE / LB / PT. The language is chosen at generation time based on your prospect's. Particularly useful for international buyers.
seo.faq4q: Can the prospect open an account on tevaxia.lu?
seo.faq4a: No. The co-branded report does not redirect to tevaxia.lu. The prospect only sees your brand — we act as tool provider, never as intermediary.

valorisation.operationalAssumptionsHint: Default values benchmarked by category (sources: STR EU, HotStats, JLL Hotels & Hospitality 2024).
valorisation.methodNote: Central value = average (capitalised EBITDA DCF, €/key comparables). Cap rate and per-key benchmarks indexed by category. References: USALI 11th ed., STR EU, JLL Hotels & Hospitality 2024. Indicative — final hotel valuation requires due-diligence audit by a certified appraiser.
valorisation.nextStepDesc: Check whether EBITDA covers the debt service.

results.hintAdr: Average Daily Rate — average price per night sold

dscr.methodNote: DSCR benchmarks: commercial bank LU/FR/BE → 1.25-1.35x; SBA 7(a) US (E-2) → 1.40x; institutional hotel financing → 1.40-1.50x. The occupancy/ADR stress test mirrors bank credit committees' practice (-10 pts / -10 % shock).
dscr.ctaDesc: Check the eligibility of your project for the US E-2 visa.

results.hintEbitda: From the Valuation tool or your projected P&L

scoreE2.warningText: This score reproduces criteria published by USCIS and consular case law (FAM 9 9 FAM 402.9-6) but does not replace a US immigration attorney's analysis. Consular decisions involve discretion. No USCIS outcome is guaranteed by simply meeting a score.

results.labelRevenu: Projected annual income (net for the family)
results.hintMinVital: US poverty guidelines × 1.5 reference by state + family composition
results.hintEmplois: Excluding investor and family — US full-time equivalents
results.fondsEngagesDesc: Signed escrow, purchase agreement with deposit, signed works contracts. A simple wire transfer intention is insufficient for USCIS.
results.hotelActifDesc: Must be a genuine active business, not merely a holding or passive investment (uncheck only for purely passive transformation projects).

exploitation.methodNote: USALI 11th ed. — Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry (AHLEI). Per-category ratios from STR EU and HotStats 2024. P&L is indicative — adapt to the management contract type (operator vs lease vs management contract).

renovation.methodNote: Tertiary Klimabonus — displayed aid rates are the average observed per item for LU tertiary buildings. Exact rates depend on the budget cycle (Klima-Pak law) and the targeted performance label. Check with myenergy before commitment.

labels.hintGainLabel: Green Key, EU Ecolabel = +1-3% RevPAR observed

revparCompset.compsetHint: Weighted average across 3-5 comparable hotels (category, size, location).
revparCompset.methodNote: Method: MPI / ARI / RGI indices = STR (Smith Travel Research) and HotStats standard — global hotel benchmark reference. Index 100 = fair share, > 100 = over-performance, < 100 = under-performance.

apiBanques.title: LU estimation API for banks
apiBanques.description: Integrate Luxembourg real estate estimation into your credit CRM, MLV/CRR workflow or mobile app. 100 municipalities, TEGOVA EVS 2025 method, response under 200 ms.
apiBanques.uc1Desc: Instant pre-valuation of the financed asset. LTV computed in real time as soon as the address is captured. Cuts approval time by 2-5 days.
apiBanques.uc2Desc: Periodic re-valuation of collaterals for MLV monitoring (CRR art. 208). Identify under-collateralised exposures before ECB audit.
apiBanques.uc3Desc: Self-service estimation in the bank's app. Generate qualified credit leads from the simple exposure of an address.
apiBanques.endpointSubtitle: REST + JSON architecture, API key authentication (header), configurable CORS.
apiBanques.method2: Charter for real estate appraisal expertise (5th ed.)
apiBanques.contactSuffix: uses the same calculation engine as the API. Test it to validate result quality before discussing integration.

seo.usageContent: Three main use cases: (1) credit pre-qualification — embed an instant estimation into the online application flow, (2) automated valuation of existing mortgage portfolios for prudential CRR LTV, (3) automated scoring of a collateral submission for a non-mortgage loan backed by real estate.
seo.tegovaContent: The API returns a Market Value estimate in the EVS 1 sense (European Valuation Standards 2025 / Charter 5th ed.). Combined methods — comparison, capitalisation, hedonic — are those recognised by the TEGOVA framework. Exportable reports detail the methodology to trace each credit decision.
seo.mlvContent: For mortgage portfolios subject to CRR (Capital Requirements Regulation), the API also provides a Mortgage Lending Value computed according to EBA guidelines. Prudential discount applied to Market Value, respecting the sustainable value principle over the loan duration.
seo.faq1q: Is the API consistent with a TEGOVA expert valuation?
seo.faq1a: The API provides a TEGOVA-compliant indicative estimate based on public data. For a binding report (secured loan, litigation), a report signed by a qualified appraiser remains necessary — but the observed gap between the two is typically below 8% on the 100 municipalities covered.
seo.faq2q: Is data stored?
seo.faq2a: No client data (precise address, prospect identity) is stored on tevaxia's side unless explicitly requested in your contract. Only technical logs (anonymised requests, return codes) are kept 90 days for billing and audit.
seo.faq3q: What update frequency for market data?
seo.faq3a: Prices per m² are updated quarterly via Observatoire de l'Habitat publications (data.public.lu). Tax scales (Bëllegen Akt, VAT, duty rates) are updated within 48 hours after publication in Mémorial A.
seo.faq4q: Can the API be used without a tevaxia.lu account?
seo.faq4a: Yes. B2B integration uses a dedicated API key and a technical agreement. Your tevaxia.lu user account remains optional and separate from your server integration.
seo.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions

seo.contextTitle: A guided journey for confident decisions
seo.contextContent: Buying in Luxembourg means juggling four questions: how much is the property worth, what are the acquisition fees, which state aids apply, and — if you plan to rent it out — what is the legal rent cap under the 5% rule. Our journey chains these four steps and automatically reuses the information you have already entered.
seo.etapesContent: 1. Estimation: price per sqm by municipality/neighbourhood, adjusted for condition, floor, outdoor, parking and energy class. 2. Acquisition fees: registration duties, Bëllegen Akt credit, 3% VAT on new primary residence, notary fees, mortgage registration. 3. Aids: the 5 layers (state, energy retrofit, private, communal, heritage). 4. 5% rule: legal rent cap if you plan to rent the property.
seo.avantagesTitle: Why a journey rather than four separate tools
seo.avantagesContent: Your negotiated price flows automatically into fees and aids. Ticking primary residence or new construction in step 2 changes the aid thresholds in step 3. The final recap computes the estimated net cost (price + fees − cash aids) so you immediately know the total capital to mobilise.
seo.limitesTitle: What the journey does not replace
seo.limitesContent: This journey provides an indicative estimate based on public data from Observatoire de l'Habitat, STATEC and Luxembourg tax scales. It does not replace a personalised loan simulation from a bank, nor a formal TEGOVA EVS 2025 valuation report for a binding transaction.
seo.faq1q: Do I need an account to use the journey?
seo.faq1a: No. The journey runs entirely in your browser. No data is sent to our servers unless you explicitly save your scenario in a logged-in account.
seo.faq2q: Does the estimation account for energy class?
seo.faq2a: Yes. Energy class adjusts the per-sqm price using hedonic coefficients observed on the Luxembourg market: a bonus for A/B, a progressive discount from class E.
seo.faq3q: Which aids are computed?
seo.faq3a: All 5 layers: state acquisition aids (Bëllegen Akt, 3% VAT relief), energy renovation (Klimabonus, Klimaprêt), private aids (housing savings premium), communal aids (variable by municipality) and heritage aids (listed properties).
seo.faq4q: Does the 5% rule apply if I live in the property myself?
seo.faq4a: No. The legal cap applies only to rented properties. If you buy for yourself, skip step 4 — it is only relevant if you plan to rent the property later.
seo.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions

seo.regle5Content: The Luxembourg law of 21 September 2006 caps annual rent at 5% of the reassessed invested capital. Invested capital is the purchase price plus any works, each reassessed with a STATEC coefficient by year, then reduced by an obsolescence discount (1% per year by default). Any rent above this cap can be judicially reduced at the tenant's request, with reimbursement of the excess.
seo.klimabonusTitle: Klimabonus: strong leverage for E/F/G classes
seo.klimabonusContent: The Klimabonus scheme covers up to 65% of energy retrofit works (insulation, heating, ventilation), with an additional CO₂ premium based on actual savings. A property upgraded from F or G to B typically sees its market value jump and its legal rent rise via the reassessed works added to invested capital.
seo.rendementTitle: Gross yield vs maximum legal yield
seo.rendementContent: Many landlords overlook that in Luxembourg the maximum gross yield is mechanically capped at 5% by law. Above this, the lease is legally exposed. Our tool compares your current rent against the legal cap in real time and alerts you if you are out of bounds — a compliance safety net to avoid disputes.
seo.syndicContent: Property managers and rental operators (10-100 units) use this tool to run portfolios on behalf of third parties: compliance checks at each lease renewal, post-works impact simulation, identification of Klimabonus-eligible units.
seo.faq1q: Is my rental data sent online?
seo.faq1a: No. All units are stored in your browser's localStorage. No data leaves your machine. If you change device, export your portfolio beforehand.
seo.faq2q: Can I apply an obsolescence rate other than 1%?
seo.faq2a: The law does not fix an exact rate. 1%/year is the usual court standard, but a landlord can argue for a lower rate if the property was recently renovated. Keep invoices — they form part of the reassessed invested capital.
seo.faq3q: How are reassessment coefficients computed?
seo.faq3a: Coefficients are taken from STATEC's construction price index publications. Our table covers every year from 1960 to today. The older the purchase year, the higher the coefficient.
seo.faq4q: Does furnished rental change the calculation?
seo.faq4a: Yes. A 10% uplift on the maximum rent is accepted for furnished rentals. Tick the option in the unit form — the legal cap is adjusted automatically.
seo.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions

seo.methodContent: RevPAR (Revenue per Available Room) = ADR × occupancy rate. Multiplied by 365 × number of rooms, it gives annual room revenue. Add F&B and other department revenue to get total revenue. Subtract operating charges (staff, energy, other opex) for GOP, then FF&E reserve (4%) for stabilised EBITDA. DCF value = EBITDA / capitalisation rate.
seo.ratiosContent: Budget (1-2★): staff 28-32%, energy 7-9%, other opex 22-26%, GOP 30-40%. Midscale (3★): staff 30-34%, GOP 28-35%. Upscale (4★): staff 32-36%, GOP 25-32%. Luxury (5★): staff 35-40%, GOP 20-28%. Ranges sourced from PKF Hotel Experts and STR Global, calibrated on the European market.
seo.capRateTitle: Capitalisation rate: the most sensitive variable
seo.capRateContent: A cap rate of 7% instead of 8% mechanically raises DCF value by 14%. Observed rates in Europe 2025: luxury 4.5-6% (Paris, Milan, Zurich), upscale 6-7.5%, midscale 7-9%, budget 8-11%. For a secondary LU/BE hotel, 7.5-9% is the prudent compromise.
seo.faq1q: What does 'stabilised EBITDA' mean?
seo.faq1a: EBITDA in cruising year post-ramp-up, after 3-5 years of operation from acquisition or repositioning. Not the EBITDA of year N-1 if abnormal (covid, works, major local event).
seo.faq2q: Why 4% FF&E reserve?
seo.faq2a: Furniture, Fixtures & Equipment: gradual replacement of furniture and equipment. 4% is the USALI 11th ed. standard. A luxury hotel may justify 5-6%, an economy motel holding at 3%.
seo.faq3q: Should I use DCF or price-per-key?
seo.faq3a: Both. The DCF approach reflects the unique economic performance of the asset. The price-per-key multiple anchors on market prices. The arithmetic mean typically sets the retained range; a gap > 30% signals an atypical asset.
seo.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions

seo.definitionTitle: DSCR: the ratio the bank looks at first
seo.definitionContent: DSCR (Debt Service Coverage Ratio) = EBITDA / (principal + annual interest). A commercial bank typically requires DSCR ≥ 1.30 on a hotel loan. SBA 7(a) US: 1.25. LBO/mezzanine: 1.50. Below 1.00, the hotel does not cover its payments — the file is rejected.
seo.stressTitle: Stress tests: real robustness of the file
seo.stressContent: A central DSCR at 1.40 may hide fragility. Three stress tests to verify: occupancy −10 pts (covid effect), ADR −10% (competitive or recession pressure), combined double shock (−25% RevPAR). If DSCR falls below 1.00 under double shock, the file is risky for the bank.
seo.ltvContent: Loan-to-Value = debt / asset value. In LU/BE hospitality, banks finance 65-75% (vs 80% in residential). The 25-35% equity can be complemented by a mezzanine loan (cost 8-12%), seller credit (negotiated with the seller) or a regional commercial fund.
seo.faq1q: Why stricter DSCR in hospitality?
seo.faq1a: Higher revenue volatility than in residential rental. A hotel depends on occupancy and ADR, both sensitive to the economic cycle. A DSCR of 1.30 provides a 23% safety margin before default — about one bad quarter.
seo.faq2q: What if DSCR is borderline (1.10-1.25)?
seo.faq2a: Three levers: increase equity (lower monthly payment), extend duration (20 → 25 years), negotiate lower rate. Caution: extending duration raises total credit cost. Additional equity remains the cleanest solution.
seo.faq3q: How to estimate EBITDA if the hotel is new?
seo.faq3a: Use the Operation USALI tool to build a 5-year P&L forecast with ramp-up (lower year 1 occupancy, progressive over 3 years). Retain the years 3-5 average as stabilised EBITDA for DSCR.
seo.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions

seo.usaliContent: The Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry, published by AHLA, has standardised hotel operating accounts globally since 1926. The 11th edition (2014) remains the worldwide reference. Structure: revenue by department (Rooms, F&B, Other), charges by department, undistributed charges (A&G, marketing, maintenance, energy), GOP, fixed charges, EBITDA.
seo.gopContent: GOP (Gross Operating Profit) is the key operator performance line. Typical GOP margins: budget 30-40%, midscale 28-35%, upscale 25-32%, luxury 20-28%. A GOP below range signals operational inefficiency (over-staffing, uncontrolled energy, high food cost). The tool automatically compares your projection to the chosen category.
seo.ffeTitle: FF&E reserve: 4% required in bank valuation
seo.ffeContent: Furniture, Fixtures & Equipment: reserve for replacing furniture, linen, TV, IT, small equipment. 4% of total revenue is the standard required by banks and USALI for any valuation. A hotel claiming EBITDA = GOP without FF&E deduction overstates its result by about 15-25%.
seo.faq1q: Is the forecast conservative or optimistic?
seo.faq1a: The tool applies average USALI ratios for the chosen category. For a prudent forecast required by a bank, increase staff by 2-3 points and lower ADR by 5%. For an aggressive investor business plan, the reverse.
seo.faq2q: How to project ramp-up of a new hotel?
seo.faq2a: Year 1: 50-55% occupancy. Year 2: 60-65%. Year 3-5: 65-75% (stabilised). ADR grows 2-3%/year via inflation + repositioning. The tool manages this curve via 'ADR growth' and 'occupancy progression' parameters.
seo.faq3q: F&B: profitable revenue or cost?
seo.faq3a: Strongly category-dependent. Budget: F&B often loss-making (breakfast included), contributes via differentiation. Midscale: F&B at break-even. Upscale/Luxury: F&B can contribute 15-25% of total GOP if the restaurant attracts clients beyond hotel guests.
seo.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions

seo.klimabonusContent: The Klimabonus scheme (law of 23 December 2022) covers energy retrofits in Luxembourg tertiary buildings. For a hotel, eligible items include insulation, HVAC, LED lighting + BMS, triple-glazed windows, heat pumps and solar thermal. Subsidy rates reach 25-62.5% depending on retrofit scope and performance target, with a CO₂ bonus tied to actual savings.
seo.tertiaireContent: For tertiary, applications go through the SME Single Window and the Ministry of Economy (not Klima-Agence for individuals). Minimum improvement thresholds (e.g. 30% primary energy gain) condition eligibility. Cost caps are higher (€250-450/m² per item) and an energy audit is systematically required.
seo.roiTitle: ROI: energy savings + RevPAR gain via label
seo.roiContent: Hotel retrofit profitability combines two effects. First: direct energy savings (20-40% of annual bill after deep retrofit). Second: observed RevPAR gain via eco-label (Green Key, EU Ecolabel, Biosphere) — +1 to +3% ADR per STR and HOTREC studies. The second effect is often the real ROI lever.
seo.faq1q: Can Klimabonus be combined with other aids?
seo.faq1a: Yes, with Klimaprêt (0% subsidised loan), regional/communal aids and energy supplier premiums. Combinations are capped at 80-100% of eligible expenses depending on schemes.
seo.faq2q: Which retrofit item offers the best ROI?
seo.faq2a: For an existing E/F-rated hotel, roof insulation + heat pump replacement typically offers the fastest ROI (6-10 years). LED + BMS is the most profitable in absolute value if electricity consumption dominates. Windows are profitable only if existing ones are severely degraded.
seo.faq3q: Does an eco-label actually raise ADR?
seo.faq3a: STR Global + Cornell Hospitality studies: Green Key or equivalent certified hotels get +2 to +4% ADR vs uncertified competitors, with a 1-2 pt occupancy premium. The effect is stronger on corporate and MICE segments (CSR criteria demanded by corporate clients).
seo.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions

seo.indicesTitle: MPI, ARI, RGI: the three fair-share indices
seo.indicesContent: MPI (Market Penetration Index) = hotel occupancy / compset occupancy × 100. Measures your volume performance. ARI (Average Rate Index) = hotel ADR / compset ADR × 100. Measures your pricing. RGI (Revenue Generation Index) = MPI × ARI / 100 ≈ hotel RevPAR / compset RevPAR × 100. The reference index for overall positioning.
seo.compsetContent: A compset (competitive set) typically groups 4-7 hotels of the same geographic segment and category. Criteria: same area (city, tourist district), same star category, same positioning (business vs leisure), comparable size. Data come from STR subscriptions or direct exchange between hoteliers (common in Luxembourg via Hotrec LU).
seo.fairShareTitle: Interpretation: fair share, price problem, commercial problem
seo.fairShareContent: RGI ≈ 100: fair share reached, market-level performance. RGI < 95 with low MPI and good ARI: distribution/commercial problem (visibility, channels, reputation). RGI < 95 with good MPI and low ARI: pricing problem (under-pricing to fill). RGI > 105: over-performance — watch the market ceiling, lever for rate increase.
seo.faq1q: Where to find compset data without STR subscription?
seo.faq1a: STR Global costs €5-20k/year depending on market. Free alternatives: Hotrec LU quarterly sharing, OTA dashboards (Booking, Expedia), bilateral agreements between local hoteliers. Chambers of commerce sometimes provide aggregated data by canton.
seo.faq2q: Recommended analysis frequency?
seo.faq2a: Monthly for a cruising hotel. Weekly in high season or during repositioning phase. Revenue managers also use daily tracking on 14-day rolling windows during critical periods (trade shows, event weekends).
seo.faq3q: My RGI is good but GOP is bad — why?
seo.faq3a: RGI measures top-line performance (revenue vs market). GOP measures operational profitability. A hotel can have excellent RGI and weak GOP if it over-staffs, overpays for energy or keeps disproportionate fixed charges. Use the Operation USALI tool to identify the problem item.
seo.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions

seo.visaTitle: The E-2 visa: an immigration tool via investment
seo.visaContent: The E-2 (Treaty Investor) visa allows citizens of treaty countries with the United States (incl. Luxembourg, France, Belgium) to obtain an indefinitely renewable work visa via substantial investment in an active American business. Acquiring a hotel or motel is a preferred route: tangible asset, job creation, active not passive operation.
seo.criteresContent: 1. Substantiality: invested capital vs total project cost (typically 50-100% if project < $500k, decreasing for larger projects). 2. At-risk: funds actually committed (escrow, signed contracts). 3. Marginality: income above US family minimum vital. 4. Real & operating: active hotel, not passive holding. 5. Job creation: US jobs created or maintained (excluding investor and family).
seo.limitContent: This tool computes an indicative score on the 5 key criteria. It does not replace consulting an E-2-specialised US immigration lawyer. Subjective elements (quality of business plan, investor professional history, tax compliance, target hotel due diligence) are not considered and remain decisive for actual visa grant.
seo.faq1q: What minimum capital for a hotel E-2 visa?
seo.faq1a: No official USCIS threshold. In practice, $100-200k is considered a floor. For a hotel/motel, typical range is $300-800k equity for an $800k-$2M project, with creation of 4-10 US jobs.
seo.faq2q: Duration and renewal of E-2 visa?
seo.faq2a: Initial visa 2-5 years depending on country (5 years for LU/FR/BE). Indefinitely renewable as long as criteria (active business, maintained jobs, committed funds) are met. Each renewal costs $200-400 + legal fees.
seo.faq3q: Can children and spouse work or study?
seo.faq3a: Spouse: can apply for a US work permit (EAD) without sector restriction. Children: can study in any US public or private school (K-12, university). At 21, children lose derived status — they must change visa.
seo.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions

seo.contextContent: Commercial leases in Luxembourg are governed by the law of 3 February 2018 and the Civil Code. Unlike residential leases, the rent is freely agreed at signing — the 5% rule capping rent does not apply. Instead, the 9-year minimum duration and the right to renewal provide strong tenant protection.
seo.indexationContent: Luxembourg has no specific commercial-lease index (no French ILC/ILAT equivalent). Parties usually reference the Consumer Price Index (CPI) published by STATEC. Clauses often cap indexation at 15-25% of the initial rent to limit drifts in high-inflation periods.
seo.dureeContent: The 3 February 2018 law sets a 9-year minimum for commercial leases. The tenant may terminate at each triennial period (3, 6, 9 years) with 6-month notice. Absent 6-month notice before term, the lease renews tacitly.
seo.faq1q: Does the 5% rule apply to commerce?
seo.faq1a: No. The 5% rule (legal rent cap based on reassessed invested capital) is specific to residential leases. For commercial leases, the initial rent is freely negotiated between landlord and tenant.
seo.faq2q: How is commercial rent indexed in LU?
seo.faq2a: Through the Consumer Price Index (CPI) published by STATEC, per the formula negotiated in the lease. Usual formula: indexed rent = initial rent × (CPI year N / CPI year of signature), often capped at +15% or +20% of the initial rent.
seo.faq3q: What is the minimum duration?
seo.faq3a: 9 years (law of 3 February 2018). Tenant may exit at each triennial period with 6-month notice. Landlord may only terminate at the 9-year term for personal use or major works, otherwise an eviction indemnity is owed.
seo.faq4q: What is the « pas-de-porte »?
seo.faq4a: A sum paid by the incoming tenant to the landlord in exchange for the right to occupy. May equal several months or years of rent. Not refundable and distinct from the security deposit (usually 3-12 months).
seo.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions

A MAPE < 10% is excellent for hotel forecasting, 10-20% acceptable, > 20% signals high volatility. MAPE does not include exogenous shocks (strikes, major events, atypical seasonality).

transparencePage.pageDescription: Methodology, coefficients, data sources and back-test results. This page is public: any user can verify the quality and limitations of our model.
transparencePage.methodoP1: The model combines a reference price per m² (by municipality or neighbourhood, from the Observatoire de l'Habitat via data.public.lu) with multiplicative adjustments reflecting the property's intrinsic characteristics. This is not an AVM (Automated Valuation Model) in the banking sense — it does not have the completeness of notarial deeds.
transparencePage.methodoP2: Each adjustment is an additive percentage (positive or negative) applied to the base price. The coefficients come from the Observatoire de l'Habitat, Spuerkeess analyses and professional valuation practice in Luxembourg.
transparencePage.srcObservatoireData: Price/m² per municipality, Lux-City neighbourhoods, hedonic model
transparencePage.srcSpuerkeessData: Energy class impact on sale price
transparencePage.srcPubliciteFonciereData: Transaction volume per municipality (number of deeds)
transparencePage.coeffDesc: Each coefficient is a percentage applied to the reference price per m². The confidence level reflects the statistical robustness of the source.
transparencePage.backtestDesc: Comparison between the actual reference price and the model's estimate. Actual prices are derived from ranges observed in the relevant municipalities.
transparencePage.limite1: Observatory data is aggregated by municipality — sub-municipal granularity (street, subdivision) is not captured.
transparencePage.limite2: Atypical properties (castles, investment buildings, architect-designed houses) are not covered by this model.
transparencePage.limite3: Micro-market effects (view, nuisances, orientation) are not modelled due to lack of structured data.
transparencePage.limite4: Reference prices reflect T-4 deeds; in a very dynamic market, a 3-6 month lag may exist.
transparencePage.limite5: This model does not replace a TEGOVA/RICS expert valuation with physical visit — it provides a quick order of magnitude.
transparencePage.engagementText: this page will be updated with each recalibration (quarterly, aligned with Observatory publications). Professional users (banks, agencies) can verify the model's reliability before integrating it into their workflow.

guideHub.introP1: Luxembourg real estate follows specific rules that often differ from neighboring countries: the 5% rent cap, the Bëllegen Akt tax credit, 7% registration fees, the 3% super-reduced VAT on housing, and the Klimabonus for energy renovation. These 12 guides summarize the essentials with references to applicable legislation (law of 21.09.2006, law of 16.05.1975, law of 03.02.2018, art. 99bis/99ter/102bis LIR).

It does not constitute legal advice — consult a notary or lawyer for any individual situation.
