fraisNotaire.title: How Much Are Notary Fees in Luxembourg?
fraisNotaire.metaDescription: Breakdown of notary fees in Luxembourg: registration duties (7% or 6%), transcription 1%, notary emoluments, mortgage costs. Full calculation.
fraisNotaire.intro: Notary fees in Luxembourg represent approximately 7 to 10% of the purchase price for an existing property and 3 to 4% for a new-build (VEFA). They consist of registration duties, transcription duties, notary emoluments and mortgage costs. Here is a breakdown of each component to accurately estimate your budget.
fraisNotaire.section1Content: Registration duties amount to 7% of the sale price for an existing property (modified law of 22 Frimaire Year VII). Since 2023, a reduced rate of 6% applies under certain conditions (property intended as primary residence, price below a threshold). Transcription duties are fixed at 1% of the sale price. For a new-build (VEFA), duties apply only to the land portion, while construction is subject to VAT (3% super-reduced rate for housing, capped at €50,000).
fraisNotaire.section2Content: Notary emoluments are regulated by the Grand Ducal Regulation of 26 November 2015. They follow a proportional and degressive scale: 1.650% up to €7,500, 1.100% from €7,500 to €15,000, 0.825% from €15,000 to €30,000, then 0.550% above. For a property at €750,000, emoluments amount to approximately €5,700. Additional formalities costs (mortgage searches, cadastral extracts, certificates) typically add €1,000 to €2,000.
fraisNotaire.section3Content: If you finance the purchase with a mortgage, the mortgage registration generates additional costs: 0.5% mortgage duty on the borrowed amount, notary emoluments for the mortgage deed (same degressive scale), and land registry tax. For a €600,000 loan, expect approximately €5,500 to €6,500 in mortgage costs. In total, a buyer financing 80% of a €750,000 property should budget approximately €65,000 to €75,000 in acquisition costs.
fraisNotaire.faq1Q: How much are notary fees for a €500,000 apartment in Luxembourg?
fraisNotaire.faq1A: For an existing apartment at €500,000, expect approximately €44,000 to €50,000 in total fees (7% registration duties + 1% transcription + notary emoluments + mortgage costs if financing).
fraisNotaire.faq2Q: Are notary fees reduced for new-build properties in Luxembourg?
fraisNotaire.faq2A: Yes, for a new-build (VEFA), registration and transcription duties apply only to the land portion. Construction is subject to 3% VAT (super-reduced rate), which significantly reduces total costs to approximately 3 to 4% of the price.

regle5Pourcent.title: How Does the 5% Rent Rule Work in Luxembourg?
regle5Pourcent.metaDescription: The 5% rule in Luxembourg limits annual rent to 5% of revalued invested capital. Explanations, calculation and STATEC coefficients.
regle5Pourcent.intro: In Luxembourg, the maximum annual rent for a dwelling cannot exceed 5% of the revalued capital invested by the landlord. This rule, established by Article 3 of the modified law of 21 September 2006 on residential leases, protects tenants against excessive rents while guaranteeing the owner a reasonable return on investment.
regle5Pourcent.section1Content: Invested capital includes the purchase price of land and construction, deed costs, transformation and improvement expenses (excluding routine maintenance). This amount is revalued at the date of rent determination using revaluation coefficients published by STATEC, which reflect changes in construction costs. For example, a property purchased for €300,000 in 2005 with a revaluation coefficient of 1.45 gives a revalued capital of €435,000.
regle5Pourcent.section2Content: The maximum annual rent is strictly capped at 5% of the revalued invested capital (Art. 3 law of 21.09.2006). Formula: max annual rent = revalued invested capital × 5%. For our example (revalued capital of €435,000), the maximum annual rent is €21,750, or €1,812.50 per month. Service charges (water, heating, common area maintenance) are not included in this cap and must be billed separately based on actual costs. If a tenant believes their rent exceeds this threshold, they can refer the matter to their municipal Rent Commission.
regle5Pourcent.section3Content: Some dwellings are exempt from the 5% rule: furnished accommodation (a supplement may apply), company housing, and leases concluded before the law came into force. For ongoing leases, the landlord can only increase rent if invested capital has been increased by improvement works. The reform under discussion (bill 8184) plans to reduce the cap from 5% to 3.5% for new leases from 2026, to better protect tenants against rising property prices.
regle5Pourcent.faq1Q: How to calculate the maximum rent in Luxembourg?
regle5Pourcent.faq1A: Multiply the revalued invested capital (purchase price + works, revalued by STATEC coefficients) by 5%. Divide by 12 to get the maximum monthly rent.
regle5Pourcent.faq2Q: Will the 5% rule change in Luxembourg?
regle5Pourcent.faq2A: Bill 8184 plans to reduce the cap from 5% to 3.5% for new leases. This reform is under parliamentary discussion and could take effect in 2026.

bellegenAkt.title: What Is the Bëllegen Akt and How to Benefit From It?
bellegenAkt.metaDescription: The Bëllegen Akt is a €20,000 tax credit (€40,000 for couples) on registration duties for purchasing a primary residence in Luxembourg.
bellegenAkt.intro: The Bëllegen Akt (literally 'affordable deed') is a tax credit granted on registration duties when purchasing a property intended as a primary residence in Luxembourg. Each buyer can benefit from a credit of up to €20,000 (i.e., €40,000 for a couple purchasing together), which significantly reduces notary costs. This mechanism is provided by Article 7 of the modified law of 22 Frimaire Year VII and the Grand Ducal Regulation of 18 December 2020.
bellegenAkt.section1Content: To benefit from the Bëllegen Akt, four conditions must be met: (1) the buyer must commit to occupying the property as a primary residence within two years of acquisition; (2) they must not own another dwelling at the time of application; (3) the property must be located in Luxembourg; (4) the credit has not already been fully used in a previous purchase. The credit is personal: each individual has a lifetime credit of €20,000. A married or civil-partnered couple purchasing together therefore accumulates €40,000.
bellegenAkt.section2Content: The credit is directly offset against registration and transcription duties due at the notarial deed. Concrete example: for a couple buying an apartment at €600,000, registration duties (7%) + transcription (1%) = €48,000. With the €40,000 Bëllegen Akt, they pay only €8,000 in duties. The notary automatically applies the credit when drafting the deed, after verification with the Registration and Domains Administration (AED). If the credit exceeds the duties owed, the balance remains available for a future purchase.
bellegenAkt.section3Content: If the buyer fails to meet the two-year occupancy commitment, the AED can claim reimbursement of the credit plus late interest. The credit does not apply to bare land without an immediate construction project. If the property is resold before the occupancy period expires, the credit may also be reclaimed. Note: the Bëllegen Akt does not reduce notary emoluments or mortgage costs, only registration and transcription duties. A partial credit is possible if part was already used in a previous acquisition.
bellegenAkt.relatedToolLabel: Simulate your fees with the Bëllegen Akt
bellegenAkt.faq1Q: Can the Bëllegen Akt be combined with other subsidies?
bellegenAkt.faq1A: Yes, the Bëllegen Akt can be combined with the 3% VAT (for new-build properties) and PRIMe House / Klimabonus subsidies for energy renovation.
bellegenAkt.faq2Q: Can the Bëllegen Akt be used for a rental investment?
bellegenAkt.faq2A: No, the Bëllegen Akt is strictly reserved for primary residence purchases. A rental investment or secondary residence does not qualify.

plusValue.title: How Are Real Estate Capital Gains Taxed in Luxembourg?
plusValue.metaDescription: Luxembourg real estate capital gains tax: speculation < 2 years, global rate, €50,000/€100,000 allowances, primary residence exemption. Art. 99ter and 102bis LIR.
plusValue.intro: In Luxembourg, capital gains from selling real estate are taxable under two distinct regimes: speculation profit if the resale occurs less than 2 years after acquisition (Art. 99bis LIR), or transfer profit if it occurs after 2 years (Art. 99ter LIR). The tax rate, allowances and exemption possibilities depend on the holding period and property use. Full exemption is possible for the personally occupied primary residence.
plusValue.section1Content: When the resale occurs within 2 years of acquisition, the capital gain qualifies as speculation profit (Art. 99bis LIR). It is fully added to taxable income and taxed at the progressive income tax rate (marginal rate up to 42% + employment fund contribution of 7 to 9%, giving a maximum effective rate of approximately 45.78%). No specific allowance applies. The capital gain is calculated as the difference between the sale price and the acquisition price increased by deed costs and improvement works.
plusValue.section2Content: When the disposal occurs more than 2 years after acquisition, the capital gain is a transfer profit (Art. 99ter LIR). It benefits from a more favourable tax regime: only one quarter of the overall tax rate applies (Art. 102bis para. 1 LIR). The acquisition price is revalued using the construction price index revaluation coefficient published by STATEC. A decennial allowance of €50,000 per person (€100,000 for a jointly taxed couple) is deducted from the taxable capital gain (Art. 130 para. 4 LIR). Example: a property purchased for €400,000 in 2010 and sold for €700,000 in 2025, with a coefficient of 1.25, gives a revalued price of €500,000 and a gross capital gain of €200,000.
plusValue.section3Content: The most common exemption concerns the primary residence: the capital gain is fully exempt if the property is the seller's primary residence at the time of disposal and they have effectively and continuously occupied it for the 5 years preceding the sale (Art. 102bis para. 3 LIR). A tax deferral (reinvestment) is possible if the sale proceeds are reinvested in another owner-occupied primary residence within 2 years (Art. 102bis para. 2 LIR). For land, a deferral regime is also possible under certain conditions. Gratuitous transfers (gift, inheritance) do not trigger capital gains taxation for the donor/deceased, but the beneficiary assumes the historical acquisition price.
plusValue.faq1Q: What is the capital gains tax rate on real estate in Luxembourg?
plusValue.faq1A: For resale within 2 years: progressive income tax rate (up to ~45.78%). After 2 years: one quarter of the overall rate, approximately 10 to 11% depending on income, after the €50,000 allowance.
plusValue.faq2Q: Is the sale of my primary residence taxable?
plusValue.faq2A: No, if you have continuously occupied the property as your primary residence for the 5 years preceding the sale, the capital gain is fully exempt (Art. 102bis para. 3 LIR).

bailHabitation.title: What Are the Rules for Residential Leases in Luxembourg?
bailHabitation.metaDescription: Residential lease in Luxembourg: law of 21.09.2006, duration, 3/6-month notice, max 3-month deposit, inventory, recoverable charges.
bailHabitation.intro: Residential leases in Luxembourg are governed by the modified law of 21 September 2006 on residential tenancy. This law strictly regulates the relationship between landlord and tenant: contract duration, termination conditions, security deposit amount, mandatory inventory and charge allocation. Rent is capped at 5% of the revalued invested capital (Art. 3). Here are the essential rules to know before signing a lease in Luxembourg.
bailHabitation.section1Content: Residential leases are concluded for an indefinite duration by default (Art. 1, law of 21.09.2006). Fixed-term leases are possible but automatically convert to indefinite-term leases if the tenant remains in occupation at expiry. The tenant may terminate at any time with 3 months' notice by registered letter (Art. 12). The landlord may only terminate with 6 months' notice and exclusively for legitimate reasons: personal need for occupation (for themselves, their spouse, ascendants or descendants), serious breach by the tenant, or demolition/reconstruction works rendering the dwelling uninhabitable. The landlord's termination must state reasons in writing or be void.
bailHabitation.section2Content: The security deposit is limited to a maximum of 3 months' rent excluding charges (Art. 5, law of 21.09.2006). It must be placed in a blocked account in the tenant's name at a Luxembourg bank. Interest earned belongs to the tenant. The deposit must be returned within a reasonable time after the lease ends, minus any established damages. Entry and exit inventories are mandatory (Art. 9). They must be drawn up jointly between the parties, preferably by an independent expert. Without an entry inventory, the tenant is presumed to have received the dwelling in good condition (Art. 1731 Civil Code). The inventory protects both parties in case of disputes about the property's condition.
bailHabitation.section3Content: Recoverable charges from the tenant are exhaustively listed in the Grand Ducal Regulation of 16 June 2015. They include: common area water/electricity/gas consumption, collective heating, routine maintenance of common areas (cleaning, gardening, elevator), municipal household waste tax, and building liability insurance. The landlord cannot pass on major structural repairs (roof, facade, structure) or management/syndic fees beyond the tenant's share. The landlord must deliver the dwelling in good condition (Art. 1719 Civil Code) and carry out major repairs. The tenant must use the dwelling responsibly, pay rent on time and carry out routine tenant repairs. In case of dispute, the tenant may refer to the municipal Rent Commission before any court action.
bailHabitation.faq1Q: What is the notice period to leave a rental in Luxembourg?
bailHabitation.faq1A: The tenant must give 3 months' notice by registered letter (Art. 12, law of 21.09.2006). The landlord must give 6 months' notice and may only terminate for legitimate reasons (personal need, serious breach, major works).
bailHabitation.faq2Q: What is the maximum rental deposit in Luxembourg?
bailHabitation.faq2A: The security deposit is capped at 3 months' rent excluding charges (Art. 5, law of 21.09.2006). It must be held in a blocked account in the tenant's name and interest earned belongs to the tenant.

copropriete.title: How Does Co-ownership Work in Luxembourg?
copropriete.metaDescription: Co-ownership in Luxembourg: law of 16.05.1975, mandatory syndic, general meetings, shares, reserve fund, voting majorities. Complete guide.
copropriete.intro: Co-ownership in Luxembourg is governed by the modified law of 16 May 1975 regulating co-ownership of built properties. Each co-owner holds a lot composed of a private part (apartment, cellar, parking) and a share of the common parts expressed in tantièmes or thousandths. Building management relies on three bodies: the syndic, the general meeting of co-owners, and the syndical council. Here is how it all works together.
copropriete.section1Content: Appointing a professional syndic is mandatory for co-ownerships with more than 5 lots (Art. 16, modified law of 16.05.1975). The syndic is the legal representative of the co-owners' association: they execute general meeting decisions, manage the budget, collect charges, maintain common areas and represent the association in court. Their mandate is limited to 3 years, renewable. The general meeting (GM) convenes at least once a year (Art. 14). It votes on the forecast budget, approves accounts, decides on works and appoints or removes the syndic. Each co-owner has voting rights proportional to their shares. Quorum is reached when present or represented co-owners hold at least 50% of shares.
copropriete.section2Content: The co-ownership regulations are a mandatory notarial deed registered at the cadastre that defines: the building's purpose, the distribution of private and common parts, the shares for each lot, rules for use of private and common parts, and charge allocation methods. Shares determine both voting rights at GMs and common charge allocation. They are calculated based on the area, location and consistency of each lot (Art. 5, law of 16.05.1975). Amending the co-ownership regulations requires a 3/4 majority of shares (Art. 14). Common parts include: structural work, roofs, staircases, entrance halls, green spaces, main pipes and collective equipment.
copropriete.section3Content: Since the 2018 reform, establishing a reserve fund is mandatory for co-ownerships with more than 5 lots. The minimum annual contribution is set by the GM and must fund future major maintenance works (facade renovation, roof, elevator). Voting majorities at GMs are graduated according to the decision's importance: simple majority (50% + 1 of present/represented shares) for routine decisions (budget, maintenance); absolute majority (50% + 1 of all shares) for improvement works; 3/4 majority of all shares for amendments to co-ownership regulations, transformation works and change of building purpose; unanimity for decisions affecting private rights (modifying share allocation, removing private equipment). The syndical council, an optional body of elected co-owners, oversees the syndic's management and prepares GMs.
copropriete.faq1Q: Is a syndic mandatory in Luxembourg co-ownerships?
copropriete.faq1A: Yes, appointing a professional syndic is mandatory for co-ownerships with more than 5 lots (Art. 16, modified law of 16.05.1975). For small co-ownerships (5 lots or fewer), management can be handled directly by the co-owners.
copropriete.faq2Q: What majority is needed to vote on works in a co-ownership?
copropriete.faq2A: Routine maintenance works require a simple majority. Improvement works require an absolute majority. Transformation works or regulation amendments require a 3/4 majority of all shares. Only decisions affecting private rights require unanimity.

klimabonus.title: What Are the Klimabonus Subsidies for Energy Renovation?
klimabonus.metaDescription: Klimabonus subsidies Luxembourg: PRIMe House, insulation/windows/heat pump/PV grants, amounts, EPC conditions, 3% VAT combination, zero-rate Klimaprêt.
klimabonus.intro: Luxembourg offers a generous set of subsidies to encourage energy renovation of existing dwellings and sustainable construction. The PRIMe House programme, revamped in 2024 under the 'Klimabonus' label, covers thermal insulation, window replacement, heat pump (HP) installation, photovoltaic (PV) panels and charging stations. These subsidies can be combined with the 3% VAT on renovation works and the zero-rate Klimaprêt loan. Here are the details on amounts, conditions and procedures.
klimabonus.section1Title: PRIMe House: Subsidies by Work Category
klimabonus.section1Content: PRIMe House / Klimabonus subsidies are granted by the Ministry of the Environment, Climate and Sustainable Development via myenergy. Key amounts (2024-2025 schedule): roof/attic insulation up to EUR 55/m²; external wall insulation up to EUR 110/m²; floor/cellar insulation up to EUR 55/m²; triple-glazed window replacement up to EUR 150/m² of glazed area; air-to-water/geothermal heat pump EUR 6,000 to 12,000; photovoltaic installation EUR 500/kWp (max 20 kWp); dual-flow ventilation with heat recovery EUR 3,000 to 5,000. Exact amounts depend on the performance achieved (improvement in EPC class) and may be increased based on social criteria. A 25% bonus is granted if the dwelling is rented at a social rent.
klimabonus.section2Content: To qualify for Klimabonus subsidies, an Energy Performance Certificate (EPC) must be carried out before and after works by an approved energy advisor. The EPC classifies the building from A (very efficient) to I (very energy-intensive) on two axes: primary energy demand (kWh/m²/year) and thermal insulation of the envelope. Subsidies require improvement of at least 2 EPC classes (e.g. from F to D). For deep renovations targeting class A or B, increased amounts are available. The pre-works EPC must not be older than 2 years. The energy audit itself is subsidised by myenergy (free advice). Works must be carried out by companies established in Luxembourg or the EU, and invoices must be settled within 4 years of the agreement in principle.
klimabonus.section3Content: Klimabonus subsidies can be combined with the super-reduced 3% VAT rate applicable to renovation and conversion works on housing in Luxembourg (limited to a ceiling of EUR 50,000 in VAT per dwelling). They are also combinable with the Klimaprêt, a zero-rate loan granted by partner banks (BCEE, BIL, Raiffeisen, etc.) to finance energy renovation works. The Klimaprêt amount can reach EUR 50,000 repayable over a maximum of 15 years, interest-free (the State covers the interest subsidy). To qualify, works must appear in the quote validated by myenergy and concern effective improvement of energy performance. In total, an owner renovating an older dwelling from class G to class B can combine EUR 30,000 to 50,000 in PRIMe House subsidies, reduced VAT (saving EUR 10,000 to 20,000) and a 0% Klimaprêt, considerably reducing the remaining cost.
klimabonus.faq1Q: Can Klimabonus subsidies be combined with 3% VAT?
klimabonus.faq1A: Yes, PRIMe House / Klimabonus subsidies are fully combinable with the 3% VAT on renovation works and with the zero-rate Klimaprêt. It is one of the most advantageous schemes in Europe for energy renovation.
klimabonus.faq2Q: How much can you get for installing a heat pump in Luxembourg?
klimabonus.faq2A: The subsidy for installing a heat pump (air-to-water or geothermal) ranges from EUR 6,000 to 12,000 depending on type and capacity, under the PRIMe House / Klimabonus programme. This amount can be increased by 25% if the dwelling is rented at a social rent. Additionally, the 3% VAT benefit and zero-rate Klimaprêt apply.

estimation.title: How to Estimate the Value of a Property in Luxembourg?
estimation.metaDescription: Property valuation Luxembourg: 3 approaches (comparison, capitalisation, DCF), data sources, key factors, hedonic model, TEGOVA EVS 2025.
estimation.intro: Estimating property value in Luxembourg requires a rigorous methodology supported by reliable data. Professionals (TEGOVA REV/TRV valuers, real estate agents) primarily use three approaches: direct comparison, income capitalisation and Discounted Cash Flow (DCF). The choice of method depends on the property type (residential, commercial, hospitality) and market data availability. The European TEGOVA EVS 2025 valuation standards govern these practices in Luxembourg.
estimation.section1Content: The direct comparison approach is most commonly used for residential properties: it involves analysing recent sale prices of similar properties in the same area, then adjusting for differences (area, floor, condition, orientation, parking). This is the preferred method when the market provides sufficient comparable references. The income capitalisation approach suits rental properties: value is obtained by dividing net annual rental income by a capitalisation rate reflecting market risk and expected return. In Luxembourg, residential capitalisation rates range between 3% and 5% depending on location. The Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) approach projects future cash flows (rents, charges, resale) over 10 to 15 years and discounts them at the required rate of return. This method is preferred for commercial and hospitality assets, in line with EVS 2025 (European Valuation Standards).
estimation.section2Content: Reliable estimation relies on quality data. In Luxembourg, the main sources are: the Housing Observatory (observatoire.liser.lu) which publishes average transaction prices by municipality and property type; STATEC which provides the residential property price index (IPIR) and construction indices; Land Registry (Registration and Domains Administration) which records notarial sale deeds with actual prices; the athome.lu property portal which aggregates market listings. For commercial properties, consulting firm reports (JLL, CBRE, Cushman & Wakefield) provide data on office rents, vacancy rates and yields. The hedonic model, used notably by LISER, decomposes a property's price based on its intrinsic characteristics (area, number of rooms, energy performance) and extrinsic factors (location, transport proximity, schools), enabling estimation of each attribute's marginal value.
estimation.section3Title: Key Valuation Factors and TEGOVA Standards
estimation.section3Content: The factors most influencing property value in Luxembourg are: location (municipality, neighbourhood, street — with price differences up to fourfold between rural areas and Luxembourg City Centre); living area and number of bedrooms; general condition and finish quality; energy performance (EPC class A to I has a growing price impact, estimated at 3 to 8% per class according to LISER studies); outdoor spaces (terrace, garden, balcony) and parking. The TEGOVA EVS 2025 standards (European Valuation Standards) define recognised bases of value: Market Value, Investment Value, Fair Value in accordance with IFRS 13. Any professional valuation report must specify the basis of value adopted, the method(s) applied, key assumptions and special conditions. In Luxembourg, TEGOVA REV (Recognised European Valuer) certified experts guarantee compliance with these standards.
estimation.faq1Q: What is the most reliable method for valuing a property in Luxembourg?
estimation.faq1A: For residential properties, the direct comparison approach is most reliable when sufficient market data exists. For rental properties, income capitalisation is recommended. TEGOVA EVS 2025 standards recommend using at least two methods and cross-checking results to strengthen estimation reliability.
estimation.faq2Q: Does the EPC affect property prices in Luxembourg?
estimation.faq2A: Yes, increasingly so. LISER studies show that improving by one EPC class (e.g. D to C) can increase property value by 3 to 8%. A dwelling in class A or B sells significantly better than one in class F or G, as buyers anticipate reduced energy costs and no upcoming renovation works.

achatNonResident.title: Can a Non-Resident Buy Property in Luxembourg?
achatNonResident.metaDescription: Buying property as a non-resident in Luxembourg: no restrictions, tax differences, Bëllegen Akt, LTV financing 70-75%, double tax treaty, rental income.
achatNonResident.intro: Yes, a non-resident can freely buy property in Luxembourg. There are no nationality or residency restrictions for acquiring real estate in the Grand Duchy. However, non-resident status brings notable differences in taxation, bank financing and rental income reporting. This guide details the essential points to know before investing from abroad.
achatNonResident.section1Title: Taxation and Bëllegen Akt for Non-Residents
achatNonResident.section1Content: Registration duties (6% of the purchase price + 1% mortgage transcription fee) apply identically to residents and non-residents. However, the Bëllegen Akt tax credit (EUR 20,000 per person, i.e. EUR 40,000 per couple) is only granted for the acquisition of a primary residence personally occupied in Luxembourg. A non-resident buying for rental investment therefore cannot benefit. Moreover, non-residents are subject to Luxembourg income tax only on Luxembourg-source income (rental income, real estate capital gains). Bilateral tax treaties (France-Luxembourg, Belgium-Luxembourg, Germany-Luxembourg, etc.) prevent double taxation: rental income is generally taxed in the country where the property is located (Luxembourg), then exempted or credited in the country of residence.
achatNonResident.section2Content: Luxembourg banks (Spuerkeess, BIL, BGL BNP Paribas, Raiffeisen, ING Luxembourg) accept to finance non-residents, but with stricter conditions. The loan-to-value (LTV) ratio is generally reduced to 70-75% for a non-resident, compared to 80-100% for a resident acquiring their primary residence. Banks will often require a 25-30% personal contribution, a first-rank mortgage on the Luxembourg property, and sometimes additional guarantees (pledged savings, personal surety). Interest rates are comparable to those offered to residents, but the maximum loan term may be reduced (20-25 years instead of 30 years). It is advisable to approach several institutions and prepare a solid file including the last 3 tax assessments, pay slips, and a detailed financing plan.
achatNonResident.section3Content: A non-resident who rents out their Luxembourg property must file a Luxembourg income tax return (model 100F for non-residents). Net rental income (rents received minus deductible expenses: loan interest, insurance, maintenance works, building depreciation at 2% per year) is taxed at Luxembourg's progressive rate. The maximum marginal rate is 42% (taxable income > EUR 200,004), plus the employment fund contribution (7-9%). Non-residents benefit from the same deductions as residents on their Luxembourg rental income: debit interest, acquisition costs (Werbungskosten), building depreciation. In case of resale, the real estate capital gain is taxed in Luxembourg according to Article 99bis L.I.R. (quarter-rate of the global rate after 2 years of ownership). The tax treaty with the country of residence prevents double taxation.
achatNonResident.faq1Q: Can a non-resident benefit from Bëllegen Akt?
achatNonResident.faq1A: No, Bëllegen Akt (EUR 20,000 tax credit per buyer on registration duties) is reserved for acquiring a primary residence personally occupied in Luxembourg. A non-resident buying for rental investment cannot benefit and pays full registration duties (6% + 1% transcription).
achatNonResident.faq2Q: What is the minimum deposit for a non-resident in Luxembourg?
achatNonResident.faq2A: Luxembourg banks generally require a personal contribution of 25-30% of the purchase price for a non-resident (LTV of 70-75%), compared to sometimes 0-20% for a resident acquiring their primary residence. This ratio varies by bank, borrower profile and property location.

tva3Pourcent.title: How to Benefit from the 3% VAT on Housing in Luxembourg?
tva3Pourcent.metaDescription: 3% VAT in Luxembourg: super-reduced rate for housing, conditions, max 400 m², AED application, VEFA, EUR 50,000 benefit cap. Complete guide.
tva3Pourcent.intro: Luxembourg applies a super-reduced VAT rate of 3% (instead of the standard 17%) on the construction, renovation and acquisition of residential housing. This preferential rate, provided by the modified VAT law and the Grand Ducal Regulation of 30 July 2002, constitutes a significant tax advantage of up to EUR 50,000 per dwelling. Here are the conditions and procedure to benefit from it.
tva3Pourcent.section1Content: The super-reduced 3% rate applies under several cumulative conditions. The dwelling must be used for residential purposes (primary or secondary residence, residential rental). The habitable surface must not exceed 400 m²; beyond this, the 17% rate applies to the excess area. The beneficiary must obtain prior authorization from the Registration, Domains and VAT Administration (AED) before work begins or the acquisition deed is signed. Works must concern the creation of housing (new construction, conversion) or the renovation/repair of an existing dwelling over 20 years old (certain services). Commercial premises, offices and garages not attached to the dwelling remain at the standard 17% rate.
tva3Pourcent.section2Content: The application for the 3% rate must be submitted to the AED (Registration, Domains and VAT Administration) before work begins. The application form (available on guichet.lu) must include housing plans, the building permit, and a cost estimate. The AED issues an approval decision specifying the maximum VAT amount eligible for the reduced rate. For a VEFA buyer (off-plan purchase), the developer invoices 3% VAT directly on the housing portion (up to 400 m²). For individual builders, tradespeople invoice at 17% and the owner claims the difference (14 points) from the AED. The VAT benefit cap is set at EUR 50,000 per dwelling, corresponding to approximately EUR 357,000 of works excluding VAT benefiting from the reduced rate.
tva3Pourcent.section3Title: Special Cases: Renovation and Rental Housing
tva3Pourcent.section3Content: The 3% VAT also applies to renovation of existing dwellings, with no minimum building age requirement for housing creation works (converting commercial premises to residential, for example). For repair and maintenance works, the dwelling must be over 20 years old and works must be carried out by a professional. Landlords also benefit from the 3% rate on properties they rent as residential housing, provided the tenant establishes their residence there. The benefit is capped at EUR 50,000 per dwelling, all works combined over the property's lifetime. Total demolition-reconstruction works are treated as new construction and benefit from the reduced rate. However, landscaping, swimming pools and other luxury exterior amenities remain at 17%.
tva3Pourcent.faq1Q: What is the 3% VAT benefit cap in Luxembourg?
tva3Pourcent.faq1A: The VAT benefit cap is EUR 50,000 per dwelling. This means the difference between 17% VAT and 3% VAT cannot exceed EUR 50,000, corresponding to approximately EUR 357,000 of works excluding VAT at the reduced rate. Beyond this, the standard 17% rate applies.
tva3Pourcent.faq2Q: Is prior authorization required for the 3% VAT?
tva3Pourcent.faq2A: Yes, a prior application must be submitted to the AED (Registration, Domains and VAT Administration) before work begins. Without this authorization, tradespeople must invoice at the standard 17% rate. The form is available on guichet.lu and must include plans, building permit and cost estimate.

bailCommercial.title: What Are the Rules for Commercial Leases in Luxembourg?
bailCommercial.metaDescription: Commercial lease in Luxembourg: law of 03.02.2018, minimum 9-year term, triennial termination, CPI STATEC indexation, key money, renewal rights. Complete guide.
bailCommercial.intro: Commercial leases in Luxembourg are governed by the law of 3 February 2018 on commercial leases, which came into force on 1 April 2018. This law profoundly modernized the legal framework for commercial leases, which previously fell solely under the Civil Code. It establishes a minimum 9-year term, a renewal right for the tenant, regulated rent indexation, and specific termination rules. This guide presents the essential rules for landlords and tenants of commercial premises in Luxembourg.
bailCommercial.section1Content: The law of 3 February 2018 imposes a minimum 9-year term for all commercial leases (Art. 2). Parties may agree on a longer term, but never shorter. The tenant benefits from a triennial termination right: they may end the lease at the expiry of each 3-year period (3, 6, 9 years) with 6 months' notice, served by extrajudicial act or registered letter (Art. 4). The landlord may only terminate during the lease term in exhaustively listed cases: serious tenant breach, landlord's personal need for business operation (under strict conditions), or total demolition/reconstruction works. Non-renewal at lease expiry must also be notified 6 months in advance. The lease renews by tacit renewal for 9 years if neither party gives notice of non-renewal.
bailCommercial.section2Content: Unlike residential leases, commercial rent is not capped by the 5% rule of invested capital (Art. 3, law of 21.09.2006 on residential leases). Rent is freely set between parties according to market conditions. Commercial rent indexation is linked to the Consumer Price Index (CPI) published by STATEC, not to a commercial rent index (ILC) as in France. The indexation clause must be expressly provided in the lease; otherwise, rent remains fixed. Rent review may be requested by either party at the end of each 3-year period if the rent no longer corresponds to actual rental value (Art. 7). Key money (pas-de-porte) is lawful in Luxembourg: the landlord may require an initial payment in exchange for the commercial advantages of the location. This amount is unregulated and freely negotiated.
bailCommercial.section3Content: The commercial tenant benefits from a lease renewal right (Art. 5, law of 03.02.2018). This right is exercised at lease expiry by notifying the landlord of a renewal request at least 6 months before the term. A landlord who refuses renewal without legitimate grounds must pay the tenant eviction compensation. This compensation covers the tenant's loss: loss of clientele, moving and reinstallation costs, compensation for loss of business goodwill. The amount is set either amicably or by a judge. The landlord may refuse renewal without compensation only for serious grounds: tenant's breach of obligations, building condition requiring demolition/reconstruction, or personal business need (the landlord must then operate the premises for at least 2 years). Assignment of the commercial lease is possible unless otherwise stipulated, but the landlord must be informed of the assignment.
bailCommercial.faq1Q: What is the minimum term of a commercial lease in Luxembourg?
bailCommercial.faq1A: The minimum term is 9 years (Art. 2, law of 3 February 2018). The tenant may terminate at each triennial period (3, 6, 9 years) with 6 months' notice. The landlord may only terminate for reasons exhaustively listed by law.
bailCommercial.faq2Q: Is commercial rent capped in Luxembourg?
bailCommercial.faq2A: No, unlike residential leases (capped at 5% of invested capital), commercial rent is freely set between parties according to market conditions. There is no legal cap. Indexation is based on the STATEC CPI if a clause provides for it in the lease.

investirHotel.title: How to Invest in a Hotel in Luxembourg?
investirHotel.metaDescription: Investing in a hotel in Luxembourg: ~130 hotels market, RevPAR, ADR, EBITDA, capitalization, price/room, bank DSCR, due diligence, Klimabonus. Complete guide.
investirHotel.intro: Luxembourg has approximately 130 classified hotels (1 to 5 stars), representing some 8,000 rooms, with an average occupancy rate of around 60-65%. The market is driven by business tourism (European institutions, financial sector), leisure tourism (Ardennes, Moselle, Luxembourg City UNESCO heritage) and the Greater Region cross-border area (250,000 cross-border workers). This guide presents key metrics, valuation approaches and due diligence steps for investing in a hotel in Luxembourg.
investirHotel.section1Content: Hotel investment analysis relies on several standardized performance indicators. RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) measures accommodation revenue per available room: it is calculated by multiplying occupancy rate by ADR (Average Daily Rate). In Luxembourg, average RevPAR ranges from EUR 60 (budget/economy) to EUR 150 (upscale/luxury) depending on category and location. Average ADR varies from EUR 80-100 (budget) to EUR 200-300 (luxury). GOP (Gross Operating Profit) represents gross operating income before management fees, rent, financial charges and taxes — typically 30-45% of total revenue for a well-managed hotel. EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization) is the basis for capitalization valuation. The IRR (internal rate of return) expected by hotel investors generally ranges from 8% to 15% depending on risk profile.
investirHotel.section2Content: Three main methods allow hotel valuation in Luxembourg. EBITDA capitalization: a multiple is applied to the hotel's stabilized EBITDA. Observed multiples in Luxembourg range from 8x to 14x depending on category (budget to luxury), location (Luxembourg City vs regions) and property condition. A well-maintained 3-star hotel in Luxembourg City may be valued at 10-12x EBITDA. The price per key approach: this method compares total transaction price to number of rooms. In Luxembourg, observed prices range from EUR 80,000-120,000/room (budget, regions) to EUR 250,000-400,000/room (upscale, Luxembourg City). The DCF (Discounted Cash Flow) method: it discounts future cash flows over 10 years with a terminal value, using a discount rate (WACC) of 7-10% reflecting hotel risk. The DSCR (Debt Service Coverage Ratio) required by Luxembourg banks ranges from 1.25x to 1.40x, which determines the maximum debt amount available.
investirHotel.section3Content: Hotel due diligence in Luxembourg must cover several essential areas. The operational aspect: analysis of the last 3-5 fiscal years (detailed P&L by department: accommodation, catering, events, spa), RevPAR, occupancy rate and ADR trends, STR (Smith Travel Research) benchmarking against the local competitive set. The real estate aspect: building structural condition, fire safety compliance (ITM — Labour and Mines Inspectorate), disability accessibility, energy assessment (Energiepass), and estimated renovation CAPEX needed. The legal aspect: verification of lease or title deed, operating permits (trade authorization, restaurant license), star classification by the Ministry of Economy, and current management/franchise contracts. On the incentives side, the Klimabonus tertiary program offers subsidies for energy renovation of commercial buildings (insulation, heat pumps, photovoltaics), and SME grants from the Ministry of Economy are available for hotel modernization investments. The Greater Region (Saarland, Rhineland-Palatinate, Lorraine, Wallonia) constitutes a natural catchment area of over 11 million inhabitants for Luxembourg hotels.
investirHotel.faq1Q: What is the average RevPAR of hotels in Luxembourg?
investirHotel.faq1A: Average RevPAR in Luxembourg ranges from EUR 60 for budget/economy hotels to EUR 150 for upscale/luxury hotels. Luxembourg City shows the highest RevPAR, driven by business tourism and European institutions. Tourist regions (Ardennes, Moselle) have more seasonal RevPAR patterns.
investirHotel.faq2Q: What DSCR do banks require for a hotel loan in Luxembourg?
investirHotel.faq2A: Luxembourg banks generally require a DSCR (Debt Service Coverage Ratio) between 1.25x and 1.40x for hotel financing. This means available cash flow after operating expenses must cover at least 1.25 to 1.40 times the annual debt service (principal + interest). A DSCR below 1.25x will make financing difficult to obtain.

iaTevaxia.title: How to use AI on tevaxia.lu
iaTevaxia.metaDescription: tevaxia.lu AI assistant: results analysis, EVS drafting, PDF extraction, Luxembourg real estate chat. Free 5/day, unlimited with your key (BYOK).
iaTevaxia.intro: tevaxia.lu embeds an AI engine specialized in Luxembourg real estate — LIR tax law, TEGOVA EVS 2025, VEFA, condominium, USALI hotel P&L, AML/KYC. Every signed-in user gets 5 free analyses per day via Cerebras (Llama 3.1 8B). For unlimited use or access to GPT-4o / Claude, you can add your own API key (BYOK) in your profile. Here's how to make the most of each feature.
iaTevaxia.section1Content: On about ten tools (estimation, EVS valuation, promoter P&L, multi-lease DCF, USALI hotel, E-2 visa score, VEFA, capital gains, property comparison, buy-vs-rent), an "Analyze with AI" button appears after each calculation. The AI receives the full context (inputs + results) and writes a professional commentary: price-level diagnostic, risk identification, ratio consistency vs Luxembourg market standards, arbitrage recommendations. Prompts are domain-specific — a promoter P&L analysis cites typical land-cost ratios (10-20% of revenue), a USALI P&L analysis compares to the expected GOP margin by category.
iaTevaxia.section2Content: The EVS Report mode on the /valorisation page embeds 8 "Draft with AI" buttons next to the narrative fields (location, environment, access, method justification, tax regime, ESG impact, reservations, uncertainty). Each button generates a formal paragraph injected directly into the field, compliant with TEGOVA standards and the Charter 5th edition, with legal references (art. 102bis LIR, IVS 103, CRREM pathway). The EVS Challenger mode on the Reconciliation tab acts as a critical auditor: it flags missing data, inconsistencies, abnormal ratios and compliance risks before signing — prefixed [BLOCKING], [MAJOR] or [MINOR].
iaTevaxia.section3Content: To pre-fill a form from an existing document (promoter budget, capital-gain deed, EPC certificate), a "Pre-fill from PDF" button triggers a vision extraction via your BYOK Anthropic (for PDFs) or OpenAI (for images) key. The model returns structured JSON that feeds the fields directly. This feature requires a BYOK API key because the free Cerebras/Groq models don't handle vision. 8 MB file limit. Currently available on /bilan-promoteur and /plus-values; other schemas (EPC) are supported by the API.
iaTevaxia.section4Content: A floating chat widget at the bottom right opens a free conversation with the Luxembourg real estate assistant. You can ask cross-cutting questions that don't fit a specific tool: "what VAT applies to mixed-use building renovation?", "what majority is needed to vote a roof refurbishment in a condominium?", "how is the Bëllegen Akt computed for a non-resident couple?". The assistant keeps multi-turn context. For B2B integrators, the 3 endpoints (`/api/v1/ai/analyze`, `/api/v1/ai/chat`, `/api/v1/ai/extract`) are accessible with a tevaxia API key (X-API-Key header) at the standard tier rate limit — full documentation at /api-docs.
iaTevaxia.faq1A: Yes, 5 analyses per day per signed-in user, at no cost. Beyond that, you can add your own Cerebras, Groq, OpenAI or Anthropic key in your profile for unlimited use. The tevaxia server uses Cerebras (Llama 3.1 8B) for the free tier, with automatic fallback to Groq if configured.
iaTevaxia.faq2Q: Is my data sent to a third party?
iaTevaxia.faq2A: Yes, requests transit through the configured AI provider (Cerebras by default, or your BYOK key). The contexts sent contain only the current calculation's inputs and results — never personal data beyond that scope. Cerebras and Groq don't store prompts for training. If you're bound by confidentiality (e.g. sensitive AML/KYC), prefer an Anthropic or OpenAI key with the enterprise terms of your choice.
iaTevaxia.faq3Q: Does the AI replace a certified expert?
iaTevaxia.faq3A: No. The AI provides drafting and analysis assistance, not a binding expert opinion. For an EVS report intended for a bank or court, the TEGOVA-certified expert remains responsible for the retained value and must review, correct and sign the report. The AI accelerates production (narrative draft, inconsistency detection) but doesn't replace human expertise or the on-site visit.

syndicBenchmark.loginPrompt: Sign in to access the cross-coownership benchmark.
syndicBenchmark.title: Portfolio benchmark
syndicBenchmark.subtitle: Compare KPIs across all coownerships managed by your firm. Percentiles are computed within your portfolio — green = top 25%, amber = middle, red = bottom 25%.
syndicBenchmark.needTwo: At least 2 coownerships are needed to produce a meaningful benchmark.
syndicBenchmark.hintChargesM2: Annual budget per sqm of total surface. Lower is better (efficiency).
syndicBenchmark.hintFundsM2: Accumulated works fund per sqm. Higher is better (reserves).
syndicBenchmark.hintRecouvrement: Percentage collected on issued calls. Target ≥ 95%.
syndicBenchmark.hintRetard: Average days between due date and payment date. Target < 7 days.
syndicBenchmark.hintScore: Composite score (0-100) based on percentiles of the 5 main KPIs in your portfolio.
syndicBenchmark.methodology: Methodology: KPIs are computed in real-time from your portfolio data. Percentiles are relative — they don't compare to a national market baseline. For broader benchmarking, contact us.

strForecast.title: 12-month STR forecast
strForecast.subtitle: Holt-Winters model with annual seasonality (m=12) applied to your monthly Airbnb/Booking reservation data. Produces a 6-24 month projection of occupancy, ADR and revenue with 95% confidence interval.
strForecast.csvHint: One line per month. Occupancy as decimal (0.72) or percent (72). Minimum 6 months, ideally 24+ months to activate seasonality.
strForecast.noRows: No valid rows detected in CSV.
strForecast.emptyState: Load your 6+ months of history (CSV or demo) to generate the forecast.
strForecast.insufficientData: Only {count} months of history — minimum 6 required for a reliable forecast.
strForecast.methodology: Methodology: triple exponential smoothing Holt-Winters additive (α=0.3, β=0.1, γ=0.3, 12-month period). 95% bands grow as √h (uncertainty increases with horizon). Revenue = occupancy × ADR × available days/month. No data is stored on server — everything runs in your browser.

observatoireHotellerie.title: Luxembourg hotel observatory
observatoireHotellerie.subtitle: National occupancy, nights, RevPAR by category. Public data STATEC + Eurostat + STR EMEA — free alternative to paid STR Global / Horwath panels.
observatoireHotellerie.chartMonthlySubtitle: Classic LU seasonality — troughs in Jan/Dec, peaks Jun-Aug
observatoireHotellerie.chartOriginSubtitle: STATEC B5200 — nights by country of residence 2025
observatoireHotellerie.sourcesNote: All data comes from public sources. No commercial partnership required.
observatoireHotellerie.disclaimer: Quarterly data published with 2-3 month lag by STATEC. Category ADRs are weighted estimates.

ais.title: Social rental management (AIS) — 75 % tax allowance
ais.subtitle: If you rent via a partner Social Real Estate Agency (AIS), you benefit from a 75 % tax allowance on rental income (art. L. 162bis LIR, tripartite agreement 03/2023). Typical savings: 40-60 % less tax.
ais.chargesHint: Loan interest + PNO insurance + condo fees + maintenance + depreciation
ais.cond1: Exclusive management mandate signed with an approved AIS (min 3 years)
ais.cond2: Capped rent set by AIS (typically -10 to -15 % vs market)
ais.cond3: Decent property (F/G energy class excluded, minimum standards)
ais.cond4: AIS selects eligible tenants and guarantees rent payment
ais.partnersSubtitle: Non-exhaustive list of approved AIS — direct contact, no tevaxia intermediary.
ais.sourcesNote: All information comes from public sources. Tevaxia aggregates info but is not partnered with AIS — contact agencies directly.
ais.disclaimer: Indicative figures. Actual allowance depends on your full tax situation. Consult a tax adviser or ACD for validation.

energyAudit.title: Guided energy audit
energyAudit.subtitle: 20 questions to diagnose your property, identify priority items and estimate a renovation plan with applicable Klimabonus.
energyAudit.question: Question {n} / {total}
energyAudit.gainTotal: Potential energy savings: {pct} % if full plan executed
energyAudit.planSubtitle: Priority 1 = urgent, 2 = important, 3 = comfort. Each item with cost range + applicable Klimabonus + estimated gain.
energyAudit.noReco: Congratulations — your property is energy-efficient. No major recommendations identified.
energyAudit.disclaimer: Indicative results based on questionnaire. For an official audit eligible for subsidies, consult myenergy.lu (CPE audit). Cost ranges are LU 2026 averages.
energyAudit.recToitureDesc: Priority #1 — roof accounts for 25-30 % of thermal losses in an uninsulated building. Mineral wool blowing or PU panels.
energyAudit.recFacadeDesc: External thermal insulation — 20-30 % losses, eliminates thermal bridges. Building permit may be needed.
energyAudit.recFenetresDesc: Triple glazing Uw ≤ 0.9 W/m²K. Strong visual impact, immediate comfort and value gain.
energyAudit.recSolDesc: Below (accessible basement) or above (during full renovation). 5-10 % savings.
energyAudit.recChauffageDesc: Air-source or geothermal HP — eligible 70 % Klimabonus if COP ≥ 3.5. Removes fuel oil (new ban 2026).
energyAudit.recVMCDesc: Mechanical ventilation with heat recovery — > 85 % efficiency, eliminates humidity/mold. Mandatory post-ETICS.
energyAudit.recECSDesc: HPWH — 60-70 % savings vs electric. Can be coupled with heating HP.
energyAudit.recPVDesc: Self-consumption + surplus export. Payback 8-12 yrs. 6 kWp = 6,000-9,000 kWh/yr in Luxembourg.

marcheForecast.title: Real estate price forecast 12-48 months
marcheForecast.subtitle: 3-scenario projection (pessimistic, central, optimistic) by commune, based on STATEC / Housing Observatory historical trends.
marcheForecast.chartNote: Dashed lines = projections. Assumes constant growth; reality includes inflections (rate crises, fiscal reform, economic cycle).
marcheForecast.methodHistorical: National history: STATEC/Observatory PRIX_MOYEN_M2 2015-2026, monthly interpolation.
marcheForecast.methodRatio: Commune adjustment: current commune/national ratio applied to history.
marcheForecast.methodProjection: Projection: compound annual growth per scenario.
marcheForecast.methodDisclaimer: Indicative tool. For official valuation use /valorisation EVS 2025 or a TEGOVA REV/TRV expert.

particulier.title: Individual guide
particulier.intro: Whether buying your home, valuing an inherited property, or simulating a first rental investment, tevaxia covers your path from A to Z with tools calibrated on the Luxembourg market.
particulier.parcoursIntro: Three steps a Luxembourg individual typically takes before signing.
particulier.parcoursStep1Desc: Enter address, area and energy class on /estimation. Indicative low/central/high value in 30s. For atypical or historical property, use /valorisation EVS 2025.
particulier.parcoursStep2Desc: /frais-acquisition gives notary + registration + Bëllegen Akt + VAT. Cross with /simulateur-aides for Klimabonus, commune aids, renovation.
particulier.parcoursStep3Desc: /achat-vs-location runs 1000 Monte-Carlo scenarios. You see the probability that buying wins based on holding period, alternative yield, and appreciation.
particulier.toolsIntro: Click to jump directly to each.
particulier.faq1q: Do I need to log in to use the tools?
particulier.faq1a: No, most public tools (estimation, fees, aids, capital gains) work without an account. An account lets you save estimates to the cloud (500 free items), receive price alerts per commune and generate signed PDF reports.
particulier.faq2q: Is the tevaxia estimate accepted by banks?
particulier.faq2a: The public estimate is indicative (MAPE ~14.7%). For a banking file, most LU banks require an EVS 2025 report signed by a TEGOVA REV/TRV certified valuer. The /valorisation module generates a compliant PDF, SHA-256 signable, publicly verifiable at /verify.
particulier.faq3q: How does the Bëllegen Akt work?
particulier.faq3a: The Bëllegen Abatement (law 09/07/2014, amended) offers a registration duty tax credit of up to €30,000 per buyer for a primary residence. /frais-acquisition computes it automatically based on your profile (resident, first-time, couple/single).
particulier.faq4q: Can I use tevaxia for property in France or Belgium?
particulier.faq4a: The methods (hedonic estimation, DCF, fees) are international, but LU-specific tools (Bëllegen Akt, Klimabonus, PAG, AIS) don't apply. For a cross-border property, use /estimation in generic mode + adjust fees per local legislation.
particulier.ctaDesc: Create a free account to save estimates, receive alerts and generate PDF reports.

expert.title: Expert / valuer guide
expert.intro: TEGOVA REV/TRV, notary, tax advisor, court expert: tevaxia gives you EVS 2025 valuation tools, a public calibrated hedonic model, and a SHA-256 signable report verifiable by your client.
expert.parcoursIntro: From mission request to signed report delivery.
expert.parcoursStep1Desc: Enter the property in /valorisation (3 templates: standard, bank MLV, judicial/estate). Auto-loads comparables via ACT + Housing Observatory.
expert.parcoursStep2Desc: Compare hedonic value (/hedonique CI 95%) + multi-tenant DCF (/dcf-multi) + ACT comparables. The module reconciles the 3 methods with configurable weighting.
expert.parcoursStep3Desc: PDF EVS 2025 export with SHA-256 block + /verify URL. Client can publicly verify report integrity without API. Immutable in DB (law 12.02.1979 art. 61-63).
expert.faq1a: Yes, /transparence shows the 14 coefficients, 95% CI, significance (ns/*/**/***), MAPE 14.7% and R². Model re-calibrated on ACT 2018-2024. You can cite this method in your report.
expert.faq2a: On issuance, the PDF is hashed and the hash stored in DB (immutable via trigger). The signature block contains hash + /verify URL + timestamp. Client pastes URL on /verify which re-hashes and compares — if matches, integrity guaranteed. No costly PKI needed.
expert.faq3a: /profil/organisation lets you create a firm, invite members (admin/member/viewer roles), share valuations. Admin stats via OrgAgencyStats: top valuers, API calls, conversion. VIES lookup on creation to pre-fill from VAT number.
expert.faq4q: Can I export to DOCX for my firm template?
expert.faq4a: The tevaxia PDF is the signable deliverable. For an editable DOCX, use 'Copy JSON' and import into your Word template via mail-merge, or contact us for custom templated DOCX export.
expert.ctaDesc: Pro account + B2B organisation for valuers: 10,000 reports cap, multi-user, agency stats.

syndic.title: Syndic / condominium guide
syndic.intro: Professional or volunteer syndic: tevaxia covers Luxembourg condominiums (law 16.05.1975 amended, bill 7763 works fund) from unit register to owner portal via GMs and accounting.
syndic.parcoursIntro: Yearly cycle, from closing to GM.
syndic.parcoursStep1Desc: /syndic/coproprietes: create a condo, enter units (area, shares, EPC class), owners (owner-occupier vs landlord). Auto charge split by shares.
syndic.parcoursStep2Desc: Generate quarterly funds calls + works fund (bill 7763: min 5% capitalisation if building > 10 years old). Accounting 11th ed. LU chart of accounts, bank exports for transfers.
syndic.parcoursStep3Desc: PDF convocation law 16.05.1975 art. 7, agenda, proxy. Signable minutes, video conference supported. 10-year archives in encrypted bucket SHA-256 + DB immutability (art. 13bis).
syndic.faq1q: Is bill 7763 on works fund integrated?
syndic.faq1a: Yes. /syndic/coproprietes/[id]/fonds-travaux computes minimum legal capitalisation (5% of annual operating budget for buildings > 10 years), multi-year works plan (PPT), and alert thresholds. When the law is enacted (expected 2026-2027), tools will switch to final parameters.
syndic.faq2q: What's the difference between LU and FR syndic?
syndic.faq2a: In LU, syndic is not mandatory if < 5 units and unanimous representation. No mandatory separate account (unlike FR ALUR law). No official ARC/ASCR. Fees are free (competition Matera/Cotoit online vs traditional firms). tevaxia tools are LU-calibrated but 11th PCMN accounting methods are standard BE/LU.
syndic.faq3q: How to send GM convocation and collect proxies?
syndic.faq3a: /syndic/procuration generates a PDF compliant with art. 7 + art. 11 law 16.05.1975 (agent, powers, time limit). Email via Resend/SendGrid (your key). The owner portal also allows co-owners to grant proxy online with Supabase auth.
syndic.faq4q: How much does tevaxia cost for a syndic?
syndic.faq4a: Free plan: 500 cloud items = fits 1-3 condos. Pro plan (SARL-S LU incorporation in progress) = 10,000 items, multi-user org_type syndic, distinct 'conseil_syndical' / 'coproprietaire' roles. Early adopter lifetime pricing for first registrants.
syndic.ctaDesc: Free up to 500 cloud items. Create an account and CSV-import units in 5 min.

hotelier.title: Hotelier guide
hotelier.intro: Hotel, motel, aparthotel, B&B operator: tevaxia combines financial analysis (valuation, DSCR, USALI) and operational PMS (bookings, 3% VAT invoicing, iCal OTA) in one LU-calibrated tool.
hotelier.parcoursIntro: From a new hotel project to daily operations.
hotelier.parcoursStep1Desc: Value your target with /hotellerie: DCF by RevPAR/ADR/occupancy, DSCR for financing, MPI/ARI/RGI benchmark. 50-point due diligence (HVS-inspired).
hotelier.parcoursStep2Desc: /pms/proprietes/nouveau with LU defaults pre-filled (3% VAT, commune tourist tax). Wizard proposes 16 rooms + 2 BAR/NR rate plans. All adjustable.
hotelier.parcoursStep3Desc: Create booking with VAT + tourist tax preview, 1-click check-in/out, PDF invoice compliant art. 61-63 VAT law, iCal RFC 5545 for Booking/Airbnb sync (read-only). 30-day ADR/RevPAR/occupancy reports.
hotelier.faq1q: Does the PMS handle multiple hotels?
hotelier.faq1a: Yes. Each user can create N properties (hotel, motel, residence…) in /pms. /hotellerie/groupe consolidates RevPAR/EBITDA/occupancy multi-hotel with owner reports. The PMS itself handles one property at a time (dedicated view per property), but all KPIs roll up to the group dashboard.
hotelier.faq2q: Can I sync with Booking and Airbnb?
hotelier.faq2a: Yes via read-only iCal RFC 5545 feed per property or per room. Paste the URL in Booking/Airbnb/Expedia extranet so they block dates. No channel manager push (intentional: avoids overbookings, no ~€100/month channel manager SaaS fees).
hotelier.faq3a: Lodging: 3% (art. 40 law 12.02.1979 + annex B). F&B: 17% standard, 14% certain meals, 8% solid meals not consumed on-site. Tourist tax: per commune, configurable per property (€3/night/adult in Luxembourg-City 2026, varies by commune).
hotelier.faq4a: Yes, USALI 11th ed. (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry, 2014). Structure: Rooms / F&B / Other Operating Departments → Total Operating Revenue → Departmental Profit → Undistributed Operating Expenses → GOP → Fixed Charges → EBITDA. FF&E reserve 4% standard.
hotelier.faq5q: How to get Green Key ESG certification?
hotelier.faq5a: /hotellerie/certifications-esg pre-diagnoses on the 29 FEE Green Key criteria + investment roadmap (LED, water management, sorting, local purchasing). The certification itself is requested from Oekocenter (LU FEE-approved association), yearly membership ~€400.
hotelier.ctaDesc: 3-step wizard with LU defaults pre-filled. No commitment, free tier 500 bookings.

investisseur.title: Investor guide
investisseur.intro: Individual or institutional investor: tevaxia equips you for DCF analysis, multi-asset portfolio tracking, acquisition vs energy retrofit arbitrage, and LU annex 190 tax export.
investisseur.parcoursIntro: From identified opportunity to post-acquisition tracking.
investisseur.parcoursStep1Desc: /dcf-multi for a rental: break options, step rents, CAPEX, IRR equity, LP/GP waterfall. /bilan-promoteur for a new build with ±10% tornado sensitivity on 8 variables.
investisseur.parcoursStep2Desc: /outils-bancaires: credit simulation with ECB/OAT/mortgage 8y rates, early repayment (LU max 6-month indemnity), 3-offer TCC comparator.
investisseur.parcoursStep3Desc: /portfolio: multi-asset with LTV, net yield, 12-month rolling cashflow, annex 190 tax export (interest, PNO, tax, management, depreciation). 180-day bin before permanent deletion (GDPR).
investisseur.faq1q: Does DCF support complex multi-tenant leases?
investisseur.faq1a: Yes, /dcf-multi handles N leases per asset with distinct dates, CPI indexation, rent-free, step rents, tenant/landlord break options, reversion. Monthly lease-by-lease view year 1, cap rate × discount rate 5×5 sensitivity. CSV portfolio import + downloadable template.
investisseur.faq2q: How to integrate energy impact in my analysis?
investisseur.faq2a: /energy/portfolio aggregates your assets' EPCs, computes CRREM stranding risk (what share of portfolio will be 'stranded' by EPBD 2030/2033/2050 deadlines) and prioritises retrofits by ROI. Crosses with Klimabonus aids.
investisseur.faq3q: Can I share an analysis with my bank or firm?
investisseur.faq3a: /profil/liens-partages: generates a read-only public link (secure token, configurable expiry, optional max_views, rate-limited anonymous comments). Your counterpart views without account. View analytics + 30d sparkline owner-side.
investisseur.faq4q: What's LU capital gains tax policy for an investor?
investisseur.faq4a: /plus-values computes taxation per art. 99bis-101 LIR. Rate = half of global rate for > 2 years holding (art. 100 LIR), €50,000 allowance every 10 years (art. 102 LIR). Full exemption for primary residence (art. 100 para. 3). Reinvestment tax deferral art. 102 LIR. SCI/SARL share transfers (art. 100 LIR): 23% half-rate if ≥ 10% + > 6 months.
investisseur.ctaDesc: Pro: 10,000 cloud items, advanced exports, multi-user org_type investor.

agence.title: Real estate agency guide
agence.intro: LU/BE agency, property manager: co-branded estimate report in 1 PDF, pro-agences CRM (contacts, interactions, tasks, documents), mandate pipeline, Athome/atHome Pro/Immotop portal-compat export.
agence.parcoursIntro: From prospect to signed mandate to closed sale.
agence.parcoursStep1Desc: /pro-agences/fiche-bien: A4 PDF with agency logo, agent contact, custom legal footer. Estimate + acquisition costs + aids per prospect profile. Turns a 1st meeting into a mandate.
agence.parcoursStep2Desc: /pro-agences/crm: contacts (prospect/lead/buyer/seller) + mandate Kanban (prospect → signed → compromise → sold) + interaction timeline (calls/emails/visits) + tasks with reminders. CSV Athome / atHome Pro / Immotop export.
agence.parcoursStep3Desc: Estimated/received commissions tracked per mandate. 30-day expiry alerts. 5-year KYC archiving (law 12.11.2004) with encrypted SHA-256 bucket. Multi-agent roles via /profil/organisation.
agence.toolsIntro: From prospecting to full commercial tracking.
agence.faq1q: Is the co-branded report compliant with LU agent law?
agence.faq1a: Yes. PDF includes agency logo + professional real estate licence number (law 28.12.1988), legal mention 'non-contractual document, indicative value'. For a mandate, /pro-agences/mandats generates clauses compliant with grand-ducal regulation 04.07.2000 (min 3-month exclusive mandate, compensation, obligations).
agence.faq2q: Can I invite multiple agents to my agency?
agence.faq2a: Yes via /profil/organisation. Roles: admin (everything), member (agent, sees own + agency mandates read-only), viewer (read-only). Top 5 agents stats, API calls per member, mandate → sale conversion. VIES lookup to pre-fill agency VAT.
agence.faq3a: /pro-agences/mandats has CSV export compat Athome Pro (official format: address, area, price, photo URLs). Bidirectional import unsupported (no public API). If you have Athome Pro account, export from tevaxia then import in a few clicks.
agence.faq4q: Is AML/KYC mandatory for an agency?
agence.faq4a: Yes. Amended law 12.11.2004 on AML-CFT makes agents subject since 2019. /aml-kyc/archives stores KYC documents (ID, income proof, UBO) in encrypted SHA-256 bucket with 5-year retention post-business relationship, integrated EU/OFAC/UN/CSSF sanctions screening.
agence.ctaDesc: Free demo, 30-day trial, no commitment. Preferential early-adopter pricing.

promoteur.title: Developer guide
promoteur.intro: Developer, builder, property dealer: operation P&L, tornado sensitivity, multi-tranche phasing, VEFA intrinsic/extrinsic GFA, CCAP/CCTP templates compliant law 08/04/2018.
promoteur.parcoursIntro: From land to delivery, with execution tracking.
promoteur.parcoursStep1Desc: /pag-pap: PAG zones, buildability (COS/CMU × zone), road setback + green space. Public geoportail.lu geocoder. /convertisseur-surfaces to go from cadastral → buildable.
promoteur.parcoursStep2Desc: /bilan-promoteur: revenue × sales period × build cost × financial costs × VAT. Tornado chart ±10% sensitivity on 8 variables (sorted by amplitude). Multi-tranche phasing with 48-month Gantt.
promoteur.parcoursStep3Desc: /vefa: staggered funds calls, construction delay (0-24 month slider) and intercalary interest surcharge, intrinsic vs extrinsic GFA (insurer, 0.3-2% premium). /estimateur-construction: STATEC material indices + CSV export per trade for competitive bidding.
promoteur.faq1q: Does operation P&L include 3% new-build VAT?
promoteur.faq1a: Yes. /bilan-promoteur automatically applies 3% VAT for dwellings destined to buyer's primary residence (law 12.02.1979 art. 43). Toggle 'VAT 17% investor' for non-PR units. 2024 housing tax credit and €50,000 / €2M gross thresholds modelled.
promoteur.faq2q: How does intrinsic vs extrinsic GFA work?
promoteur.faq2a: /vefa separates both: extrinsic (insurer or bank guarantees if developer defaults, 0.3-2% of revenue premium) vs intrinsic (developer equity deposited with notary). Extrinsic is mandatory to sell VEFA in LU (law 28.12.1976 art. 2-11). Tool computes both costs for arbitration.
promoteur.faq3a: Yes, /estimateur-construction shows STATEC Construction Cost Index (ICV) with YoY for 6 materials: steel, concrete, wood, insulation, aluminium, copper. Quarterly update. Batiprix 2026 integrated for unit prices per trade.
promoteur.faq4q: Can I share the P&L with my banker?
promoteur.faq4a: Yes, /profil/liens-partages generates a read-only public link with secure token. Banker sees P&L without tevaxia account. Print/PDF button on viewer side. Analytics: you see view count and last consultation.
promoteur.ctaDesc: CSV import of actual costs, month-by-month execution tracking, margin overrun alert.

api.title: API integrator guide
api.intro: PropTech, bank, fintech: integrate MLV valuation, hedonic estimation and LU fees into your workflows via public REST API. OpenAPI 3.1, self-managed keys, transparent quotas, HMAC-signed webhooks.
api.parcoursStep1Desc: /profil/api: create a key with scope (estimation, mlv, dcf, fees), monthly quota, IP allowlist. Manual rotation, instant revocation. Full logs (call, latency, error) visible in real-time.
api.parcoursStep2Desc: /api-docs: OpenAPI 3.1 with Try-it-out, cURL examples, request/response schemas. Available endpoints: POST /api/v1/estimation, /api/v1/mlv, /api/v1/propcalc/{yield,cashflow,mortgage,fees}, /api/v1/ai/analyze, batch /api/v1/estimation/batch.
api.parcoursStep3Desc: Configure a webhook to receive events (estimation complete, report signed) with HMAC-SHA256 signature in X-Tevaxia-Signature. Test endpoint: /api/v1/webhooks/test.
api.faq1q: What are the quotas and pricing?
api.faq1a: Free tier: 100 requests/day (dev/test usage). Pro tier (LU SARL-S incorporation ongoing): 10,000 requests/month, 100 req/min rate limit. Enterprise: custom quotas, 99.9% SLA, dedicated support. No per-request billing (flat-rate pricing).
api.faq2q: Is there a TypeScript / Python SDK?
api.faq2a: No official SDK yet. API is standard REST (JSON, Bearer auth) usable with any HTTP client: fetch, axios, requests, httpx. SDK will be published if call volume > 1M/month. OpenAPI schemas importable in Postman / Insomnia.
api.faq3a: PDF reports (EVS valuation) include SHA-256 signature verifiable at /verify. JSON responses aren't signed by default (client already authenticates with API key). On Enterprise request: JWT response signature possible.
api.faq4a: Hedonic model is public: coefficients, 95% CI, MAPE 14.7%, R² published on /transparence. DCF/MLV algorithms follow TEGOVA EVS 2025 + EBA/CRR standards. Client source code progressively open-source (pure calculation libs on GitHub forthcoming). Backend proprietary.
api.ctaDesc: Free API key in 2 min, OpenAPI documentation + playground, support via Intercom.

str_operator.title: Short-term rental guide
str_operator.intro: Airbnb, Booking, STR concierge operator: LU profitability calculators, EU 2024/1028 regulation compliance, 3-month/year LU licensing threshold, long vs short-term arbitrage.
str_operator.parcoursIntro: From analysing an STR property to EU 2024/1028 compliance.
str_operator.parcoursStep1Desc: /str/forecast: 12-month forecast (nights rented × ADR × occupancy rate), LU seasonality (low winter / August Schueberfouer / MICE Sept-June). Occupancy ±10pp sensitivity. LU Hotel Observatory benchmarks.
str_operator.parcoursStep2Desc: /str: compares STR yield (Airbnb) vs classical lease with net charges (concierge ~20%, cleaning fees, furniture depreciation, vacancy). For Luxembourg-City: break-even occupancy often > 55% to beat a €1200/month lease.
str_operator.parcoursStep3Desc: /str/compliance: EU unified Airbnb/Booking registry (in force mid-2026, mandatory commune data transmission). In LU: 3-month/year threshold beyond which lodging licence required. Exportable PDF dossier for tax authority.
str_operator.faq1a: Mandatory registration in a unified European register (member state-issued registration number), automatic transmission of rental data to authorities (nights, amount), visible registration number on Airbnb/Booking. Progressive entry into force mid-2026. /str/compliance tracks LU transposition.
str_operator.faq2q: Must I declare STR income to IRL?
str_operator.faq2a: Yes, as BIC income (accessory commercial activity if < €23k/year gross, professional above). VAT: franchise < €35k/year. Commune tourist tax: due from 1 night. For non-resident expats, LU-France/BE/DE tax treaty with anti-abuse provision. /plus-values handles sale of an STR property per art. 102bis LIR.
str_operator.faq3a: Beyond 90 cumulative days/year of short-term rental of a non-primary-residence property in Luxembourg-City, commercial lodging licence required (law 03.11.1998 + commune regulation). Chamber of commerce registration, ICC + IRC + 3% lodging VAT + tourist tax + commune GM. /str/compliance alerts at 80% of threshold.
str_operator.faq4q: Dynamic pricing like PriceLabs / Wheelhouse integrated?
str_operator.faq4a: Not yet. /str/forecast does static seasonal forecast. For real-time dynamic pricing with market competition, third-party solutions PriceLabs ($19.99/listing), Wheelhouse, Beyond Pricing. Our roadmap includes a 'smart pricing' module based on STATEC Observatory + STR EMEA for 2027.
str_operator.ctaDesc: Free 12-month forecast + EU 2024/1028 compliance + LU benchmark.

compliance.title: Legal compliance
compliance.statusBody: tevaxia is a Factur-X preparation tool. PDF/A-3 + XML files compliant with EN 16931 are generated locally; you import them into an accredited dematerialisation platform (PDP) or into the Public Invoicing Portal (PPF) directly. tevaxia neither receives nor transits invoices. This separation keeps you free to choose your platform — you can switch at any time.

pricing.subtitle: Factur-X preparation is an integral part of the platform. No premium module, no per-invoice billing — issuance, history and 5 business templates are available on every account.
features: 5 real estate templates (landlord, syndic, hotel, commercial lease, valuer)
Sequential numbering compliant with Art. 242 nonies A CGI

q1.a: No. tevaxia is a Factur-X preparation tool. For reception and transit to the PPF, you must go through an accredited PDP (official list on impots.gouv.fr) or through the PPF directly. This separation is deliberate: you remain free to choose your PDP and can switch at any time.

q2.q: How do I use tevaxia with a dematerialisation platform?
q2.a: Create the invoice in tevaxia (form + business template), download the Factur-X file (PDF/A-3 with embedded XML), then import it into your dematerialisation platform. All DGFiP-accredited PDPs accept the standard Factur-X format.

q3.q: Can I use tevaxia to comply by 01/09/2026?
q3.a: tevaxia generates EN 16931-compliant Factur-X. For full compliance with the French reform, a reception solution (accredited PDP or PPF) is also required. Our tool covers the issuance side; mandatory reception from 01/09/2026 is handled separately.

q4.q: Is Luxembourg affected by the French reform?
q4.a: No, the 01/09/2026 reform only applies to French VAT-registered entities. Luxembourg has its own e-invoicing schedule via Peppol BIS Billing 3.0 (mandatory B2G, B2B in progress). tevaxia supports both standards.

q5.q: Why use a file rather than a direct API?
q5.a: Dematerialisation platform APIs change regularly and dedicated integrations require continuous maintenance. A standard compliant file can be imported manually or via your platform's own API depending on your preference, without dependency on a technical partnership.

q6.q: Is the data stored in the EU?
q6.a: Invoices are generated locally in the browser and downloaded directly. For signed-in users, the 12-month history is stored on Supabase (AWS eu-central-1, Frankfurt). See the Privacy Policy.

strComplianceEu.title: EU STR Register 2024/1028
strComplianceEu.subtitle: Preparation of the registration file for the single European short-term rental register. Regulation (EU) 2024/1028 — entry into force mid-2026. Obligations: mandatory identification number on all listings, transmission of nights to municipalities.

regNumber.label: Pre-filled identification number
regNumber.note: Indicative format — to be validated by your municipality upon official submission. Final format imposed by the Ministry of Tourism.

fields.primaryResidence: The property is my primary residence

strComplianceEu.warnLicense: More than 90 days/year = LU accommodation license required.

timing.label: EU Regulation 2024/1028 timing:
timing.body: entry into force mid-2026. Each member state must set up its national register. For Luxembourg, the Ministry of Tourism will likely be the competent authority (to be confirmed in Mémorial A). Platforms (Airbnb, Booking) will have to transmit per-property nights to authorities.

strPortefeuille.subtitle: Multi-property dashboard for STR concierge or investor (10-50 units). Aggregated ADR/occupancy/revenue, LU license status (90-day threshold), EU 2024/1028 registration.

verify.pageDesc: Verify the authenticity of a valuation report signed via tevaxia.lu. Enter the SHA-256 hash printed on the report to confirm it has not been altered since the valuer's signature.
verify.noService: Verification service not configured on this environment.
verify.errHashInvalid: Invalid hash: must be a SHA-256 hexadecimal value (64 characters).
verify.unknownDesc: This hash does not match any report signed via tevaxia.lu. The document may have been modified since signing, or it was never signed.
verify.revokedDesc: This signature was revoked on {date}. The report is no longer considered valid.
verify.authenticDesc: This hash matches a report signed via tevaxia.lu. The data has not been modified since signing.
verify.limitsBody: this mechanism offers a time-stamped audit trail but does not have the value of a qualified eIDAS electronic signature (LuxTrust, QTSP). For stronger legal enforceability, use an accredited QTSP. The hash only proves that the data has not been modified since the moment of the signature recorded here.
